1.2: Kenan (sorrow), Mahalalel (the blessed of God), Jared (descended),
1.3: Enoch (teaching), Methuselah (his death shall bring), Lamech, Noah (lamenting).
1.4: The sons of Noah (comfort): Shem (name), Ham (hot), and Japheth (expand).
1.5: The sons of Japheth (expand): Gomer (complete), Magog (land of Gog), Madai (middle land), Javan (Greece), Tubal (brought), Meshech (drawing out), and Tiras (desire).
1.6: The sons of Gomer (complete): Ashkenaz, Riphath [Diphath], and Togarmah (You will break her).
1.7: And the sons of Javan (Greece): Elishah (God saves), Tarshish (yellow jasper), the Kittites (bruisers), and the Rodanites (leaders).
1.8: The sons of Ham (hot): Cush (black), Mizraim (land of bondage), Put (a bow), and Canaan (lowland).
1.9: The sons of Cush (black): Seba (drink you), Havilah (circle), Sabta (Sabtah), Raamah (horse's mane), and Sabteca (striking). The sons of Raamah: Sheba (seven) and Dedan (low country).
1.10: Cush (black) was the father of Nimrod (rebellion), who began to be a mighty one (who established himself as a mighty warrior) on the earth.
1.11: Mizraim (land of bondage) was the father of the Ludites (to the firebrands), the Anamites (affliction of the waters), the Lehabites (flames), the Naphtuhites (openings),
1.12: the Pathrusites, the Casluhites (fortified) (from whom the Philistines (immigrants) came), and the Caphtorites.
1.13: And Canaan (lowland) was the father of Sidon (hunting) his firstborn (of the Sidonians, the foremost), and of the Hittites \and of Heth/,
1.14: the Jebusites (Jerusalemites), the Amorites (sayers), the Girgashites (dwelling on clay),
1.15: the Hivites (villagers), the Arkites, the Sinites,
1.16: the Arvadites (I shall break loose), the Zemarites, and the Hamathites.
1.17: The sons of Shem (name): Elam (eternity), Asshur (a step), Arphaxad (I fail as the breast), Lud (strife), and Aram. The sons of Aram: Uz (wooded), Hul (circle), Gether (fear), and Meshech (Mash).
1.18: Arphaxad (I fail as the breast) was the father of Shelah (sprout), and Shelah was the father of Eber (the region beyond).
1.19: Two sons were born to Eber (the region beyond): One was named Peleg (division), because in his days the earth was divided, and his brother was named Joktan (smallness).
1.20: And Joktan (smallness) was the father of Almodad (not measured), Sheleph (a drawing forth), Hazarmaveth (village of death), Jerah (new moon),
1.21: Hadoram (noble honor), Uzal (I shall be flooded), Diklah (palm grove),
1.22: Obal [Ebal], Abimael (my father is God), Sheba (seven),
1.23: Ophir (reducing to ashes), Havilah (circle), and Jobab (a desert). All these were sons of Joktan (smallness).
1.27: and Abram (exalted father) (that is, Abraham (father of many)).
1.28: The sons of Abraham (father of many) were Isaac (he laughs) and Ishmael (God hears).
1.29: These are their genealogies: Nebaioth (heights) the firstborn of Ishmael (God hears), then Kedar (dark), Adbeel (chastened of God), Mibsam (sweet odor),
1.30: Mishma (a hearing), Dumah (silence), Massa (burden), Hadad (mighty), Tema (desert),
1.31: Jetur (enclosed), Naphish (refreshment), and Kedemah (original). These were the sons of Ishmael (God hears).
1.32: The sons born to Keturah (incense), Abraham’s (father of many) concubine: Zimran (musician), Jokshan (snarer), Medan (contention), Midian (strife), Ishbak (he releases), and Shuah (wealth). The sons of Jokshan: Sheba (seven) and Dedan (low country).
1.33: The sons of Midian (strife): Ephah (gloomy), Epher (a calf), Hanoch (dedicated), Abida (my father knows), and Eldaah (God has known). All of these were Keturah’s (incense) sons.
1.34: Abraham (father of many) was the father of Isaac (he laughs). The sons of Isaac: Esau (hairy) and Israel (he wrestles with God).
1.35: The sons of Esau (hairy): Eliphaz (my God is gold), Reuel (friend of God), Jeush (He hurries to aid), Jalam (concealed), and Korah (bald).
1.36: The sons of Eliphaz (my God is gold): Teman (south), Omar (speaker / eloquent), Zepho [Zephi], Gatam (a burnt valley), and Kenaz (hunter); and by Timna (restrained), Amalek (dweller in a valley).
1.37: The sons of Reuel (friend of God): Nahath (rest), Zerah (rising), Shammah (astonishment), and Mizzah (fear).
1.38: The sons of Seir (shaggy): Lotan (covering), Shobal (flowing), Zibeon (dyed), Anah (answer), Dishon (thresher), Ezer (treasure), and Dishan.
1.39: The sons of Lotan (covering): Hori (cave dweller) and Homam (Hemam). Timna (restrained) was Lotan’s sister.
1.40: The sons of Shobal (flowing): Alvan [Alian], Manahath (rest), Ebal (stone), Shepho [Shephi], and Onam (vigorous). The sons of Zibeon (dyed): Aiah (falcon) and Anah (answer).
1.41: The son \sons/ of Anah (answer): Dishon (thresher). The sons of Dishon: Hemdan [Hamran], Eshban (fire of discernment), Ithran (advantage), and Cheran (lyre).
1.42: The sons of Ezer (treasure): Bilhan (their decrepitude), Zaavan (troubled), and Akan [Jaakan]. The sons of Dishan (thresher): Uz (wooded) and Aran (joyous).
1.43: These are the kings who reigned in the land of Edom (red) before any king reigned over the Israelites (before an Israelite king ruled over them): Bela (destruction) son of Beor (burning). His city was named Dinhabah (give judgement).
1.44: When Bela (destruction) died, Jobab (a desert) son of Zerah (rising) from Bozrah (sheepfold / fortress) reigned in his place.
1.45: When Jobab (a desert) died, Husham (haste) from the land of the Temanites reigned in his place.
1.46: When Husham (haste) died, Hadad (mighty) son of Bedad (solitary), who defeated Midian (strife) in the country of Moab (of his father), reigned in his place. And the name of his city was Avith (ruins).
1.47: When Hadad (mighty) died, Samlah (garment) from Masrekah (vineyard of noble vines) reigned in his place.
1.48: When Samlah (garment) died, Shaul (desired) from Rehoboth (broad places) on the Euphrates \the River/ reigned in his place.
1.49: When Shaul (desired) died, Baal-hanan (Baal is gracious) son of Achbor (mouse) reigned in his place.
1.50: When Baal-hanan (Baal is gracious) died, Hadad (mighty) reigned in his place. His city was named Pau [Pai], and his wife’s name was Mehetabel (favored of God) daughter of Matred (pushing forward), the daughter of Me-zahab (waters of gold).
1.51: Then Hadad (mighty) died. Now the chiefs of Edom were (red) Timna (restrained), Alvah (evil), Jetheth (a nail),
1.52: Oholibamah (tent of the high place), Elah (An oak), Pinon (darkness),
1.54: Magdiel (prince of God), and Iram (belonging to a city). These were the chiefs of Edom (red).

2.1: These were the sons of Israel (he wrestles with God): Reuben (behold a son), Simeon (heard), Levi (joined to), Judah (praised), Issachar (there is recompense), Zebulun (exalted),
2.2: Dan (He has judged), Joseph (YHWH has added), Benjamin (son of the right hand), Naphtali (wrestling), Gad (troop), and Asher (happy).
2.3: The sons of Judah (praised): Er (awake), Onan (strong), and Shelah (a petition). These three were born to him by Bath-shua (wealth) the Canaanite (merchant). Er, Judah’s firstborn, was wicked in the sight of the LORD {YHWH}. So the LORD put him to death.
2.4: Tamar (palm-tree), Judah’s daughter-in-law, bore to him Perez (breaking out) and Zerah (rising). Judah (praised) had five sons in all.
2.5: The sons of Perez (breaking out): Hezron (surrounded by a wall) and Hamul (spared).
2.6: The sons of Zerah (rising): Zimri (Zabdi), Ethan (enduring), Heman (faithful), Calcol (sustaining), and Dara [Darda] five in all.
2.7: The son \sons/ of Carmi (my vineyard): Achar (troubler), who brought trouble upon Israel (he wrestles with God) by violating the ban on devoted things.
2.8: The son of Ethan (enduring): Azariah (YHWH has helped).
2.9: The sons who were born to Hezron (surrounded by a wall): Jerahmeel (may God have pity), Ram (exalted), and Caleb {Chelubai}.
2.10: Ram (exalted) was the father of Amminadab (my kinsman is noble), and Amminadab was the father of Nahshon (enchanter), a leader of the descendants of Judah (praised).
2.11: Nahshon (enchanter) was the father of Salmon (garment), and Salmon was the father of Boaz (fleetness).
2.12: Boaz (fleetness) was the father of Obed (serving), and Obed was the father of Jesse (I possess).
2.13: Jesse (I possess) was the father of Eliab (God is father) his firstborn; Abinadab (my father is willing) was born second, Shimea (Shimei) third,
2.14: Nethanel (given of God) fourth, Raddai (trampling) fifth,
2.15: Ozem (I shall hasten them) sixth, and David (beloved) seventh.
2.16: Their sisters were Zeruiah (balsam) and Abigail (my father is joy). And the three sons of Zeruiah were Abishai (my father is a gift), Joab (YHWH is father), and Asahel (God-made).
2.17: Abigail (my father is joy) was the mother of Amasa (burden), whose father was Jether (Ithra) the Ishmaelite.
2.18: Caleb (dog) son of Hezron (surrounded by a wall) had children by his wife Azubah (forsaken) and by Jerioth (curtains). These were the sons of Azubah: Jesher (upright), Shobab (rebellious), and Ardon (subduer).
2.19: When Azubah (forsaken) died, Caleb (dog) married Ephrath (Ephrathah), who bore to him Hur (hole).
2.20: Hur (hole) was the father of Uri (fiery), and Uri was the father of Bezalel (in the shadow of God).
2.21: Later, Hezron (surrounded by a wall) slept with the daughter of Machir (sold) the father of Gilead (rocky region). He had married her when he was sixty years old, and she bore to him Segub (exalted).
2.22: Segub (exalted) was the father of Jair (he enlightens), who had twenty-three cities in the land of Gilead (rocky region).
2.23: But Geshur (proud beholder) and Aram (exalted) captured Havvoth-jair (villages of Jair), along with Kenath (possession) and its sixty surrounding villages. All these were descendants of Machir (sold) the father of Gilead (rocky region).
2.24: After Hezron (surrounded by a wall) died in Caleb-ephrathah (ash heap), his wife Abijah (YHWH is my father) bore to him Ashhur (black) the father (the founder) of Tekoa (a stockade).
2.25: The sons of Jerahmeel (may God have pity) the firstborn of Hezron (surrounded by a wall): Ram (exalted) his firstborn, Bunah (intelligence), Oren (cedar), Ozem (I shall hasten them), and Ahijah (brother of Yah).
2.26: Jerahmeel (may God have pity) had another wife named Atarah (a crown), who was the mother of Onam (vigorous).
2.27: The sons of Ram (exalted) the firstborn of Jerahmeel (may God have pity): Maaz (wrath), Jamin (right hand), and Eker (offspring).
2.28: The sons of Onam (vigorous): Shammai (desolate) and Jada (He knows). The sons of Shammai: Nadab (generous) and Abishur (my father is a wall).
2.29: Abishur’s (my father is a wall) wife was named Abihail (my father is might), and she bore to him Ahban (brother of an understanding one) and Molid (begetter).
2.30: The sons of Nadab (generous): Seled (exultation) and Appaim (nostrils). Seled died without children.
2.31: The son \sons/ of Appaim (nostrils): Ishi (He saves me). The son of Ishi: Sheshan (noble). The son of Sheshan: Ahlai (would that!).
2.32: The sons of Jada (He knows) the brother of Shammai (desolate): Jether (abundance) and Jonathan (YHWH has given). Jether died without children.
2.33: The sons of Jonathan (YHWH has given): Peleth (swiftness) and Zaza (brightness / fulness). These were the descendants of Jerahmeel (may God have pity).
2.34: Sheshan (noble) had no sons, but only daughters. He also had an Egyptian (double straits) servant named Jarha (the month of sweeping away).
2.35: Sheshan (noble) gave his daughter in marriage to his servant Jarha (the month of sweeping away), and she bore to him Attai (opportune).
2.36: Attai (opportune) was the father of Nathan (giver), Nathan was the father of Zabad (he endows),
2.37: Zabad (he endows) was the father of Ephlal (intercessor), Ephlal was the father of Obed (serving),
2.38: Obed (serving) was the father of Jehu (YHWH is He), Jehu was the father of Azariah (YHWH has helped),
2.39: Azariah (YHWH has helped) was the father of Helez (he has saved), Helez was the father of Elasah (God has made),
2.40: Elasah (God has made) was the father of Sismai (water crane / swallow), Sismai was the father of Shallum (retribution),
2.41: Shallum (retribution) was the father of Jekamiah, and Jekamiah (YHWH raises) was the father of Elishama (my God has heard).
2.42: The sons of Caleb (dog) the brother of Jerahmeel (may God have pity): Mesha [Mareshah] his firstborn, who was the father of Ziph (battlement), and Mareshah (crest of a hill) his second son, who was the father of Hebron (association).
2.43: The sons of Hebron (association): Korah (bald), Tappuah (the apple city), Rekem (variegation), and Shema (hear).
2.44: Shema (hear) was the father of Raham (belly) the father of Jorkeam (the people empty), and Rekem (variegation) was the father of Shammai (desolate).
2.45: The son of Shammai (desolate) was Maon (habitation), and Maon was the father of Beth-zur (house of the rock).
2.46: Caleb’s (dog) concubine Ephah (gloomy) was the mother of Haran (mountaineer), Moza (fountain), and Gazez (shearer). Haran was the father of Gazez.
2.47: The sons of Jahdai: Regem (friend), Jotham (YHWH is perfect), Geshan (lump), Pelet (deliverance), Ephah (gloomy), and Shaaph (division).
2.48: Caleb’s (dog) concubine Maacah (oppression) was the mother of Sheber (breaking) and Tirhanah (favor).
2.49: She was also the mother of Shaaph (division) father of Madmannah (dunghill), and of Sheva (YHWH contends) father of Machbenah (bond) and Gibea (hill). Caleb’s (dog) daughter was Acsah (anklet).
2.50: These were the descendants of Caleb (dog). The sons [son] of Hur (hole) the firstborn of Ephrathah (Ephrath): Shobal (flowing) the father of Kiriath-jearim (city of forests),
2.51: Salma (garment) the father of Bethlehem (house of bread), and Hareph (a plucking off) the father of Beth-gader (house of the wall).
2.52: These were the descendants of Shobal (flowing) the father of Kiriath-jearim (city of forests): Haroeh (vision), half the Manahathites (rest),
2.53: and the clans of Kiriath-jearim (city of forests)—the Ithrites (a remnant), Puthites (openess), Shumathites (the exalted), and Mishraites (touching evil). From these descended the Zorathites and Eshtaolites (I will be entreated).
2.54: The descendants of Salma (garment): Bethlehem (house of bread), the Netophathites, Atroth-beth-joab (crowns of the house of Joab), half the Manahathites (rest), the Zorites,
2.55: and the clans of the scribes (of the Sopherites) who lived at Jabez (sorrow)—the Tirathites (men of the gate), Shimeathites, and Sucathites (bush-men). These are the Kenites (smiths) who came from Hammath (hot spring), the father of the house of Rechab (the father of Beth-rekab).

3.1: These were the sons of David (beloved) who were born to him in Hebron (association): The firstborn was Amnon (faithful) by Ahinoam (my brother is delight) of Jezreel; the second was Daniel (God is my judge) by Abigail (my father is joy) of Carmel;
3.2: the third was Absalom (my father is peace) the son of Maacah (oppression) daughter of King Talmai (furrowed) of Geshur (proud beholder); the fourth was Adonijah (my lord is YHWH) the son of Haggith (festive);
3.3: the fifth was Shephatiah (YHWH has judged) by Abital (my father is the dew); and the sixth was Ithream (profit of the people) by his wife Eglah (a heifer).
3.4: These six sons were born to David in Hebron (association), where he reigned seven years and six months. And David reigned in Jerusalem (city of peace) thirty-three years,
3.5: and these sons were born to him in Jerusalem (city of peace): Shimea (Shammua), Shobab (rebellious), Nathan (giver), and Solomon (peace). These four were born to him by Bathsheba [Bath-shua] daughter of Ammiel (Eliam).
3.6: David’s other sons were Ibhar, Elishua [Elishama], Eliphelet (God is deliverance),
3.8: Elishama (my God has heard), Eliada (God knows), and Eliphelet (God is deliverance)—nine in all.
3.9: These were all the sons of David (beloved), besides the sons by his concubines. And Tamar (palm-tree) was their sister.
3.10: Solomon’s (peace) son was Rehoboam (a people has enlarged): Abijah was (YHWH is my father) his son, Asa (healer) his son, Jehoshaphat (YHWH has judged) his son,
3.11: Joram (Jehoram) his son, Ahaziah (YHWH holds) his son, Joash (given by the Lord) his son,
3.12: Amaziah (YHWH is mighty) his son, Azariah (Uzziah) his son, Jotham (YHWH is perfect) his son,
3.13: Ahaz (he has grasped) his son, Hezekiah (YHWH is my strength) his son, Manasseh (causing to forget) his son,
3.14: Amon (skilled workman) his son, and Josiah (whom YHWH heals) his son.
3.15: The sons of Josiah (whom YHWH heals): Johanan (YHWH has graced) was the firstborn, Jehoiakim (YHWH raises up) the second, Zedekiah (YHWH is righteous) the third, and Shallum (Jehoahaz) the fourth.
3.16: The successors of Jehoiakim (YHWH raises up): Jeconiah (Jehoiachin) his son, and Zedekiah (YHWH is righteous).
3.17: The descendants of Jeconiah (YHWH will establish) the captive: Shealtiel (I have asked of God) his son,
3.18: Malchiram (my king is high), Pedaiah (YHWH has ransomed), Shenazzar (splendid leader), Jekamiah (YHWH raises), Hoshama (whom YHWH hears), and Nedabiah (whom YHWH impels).
3.19: The sons of Pedaiah (YHWH has ransomed): Zerubbabel (sown in Babylon) and Shimei (renowned). The children of Zerubbabel: Meshullam (friend) and Hananiah (God has favored), their sister Shelomith (peaceful),
3.20: and five others: Hashubah (consideration), Ohel (tent), Berechiah (YHWH blesses), Hasadiah (YHWH has been faithful), and Jushab-hesed (whose love is returned).
3.21: The descendants of Hananiah (God has favored): Pelatiah (YHWH delivers), Jeshaiah (YHWH has saved), and the sons of Rephaiah (healed of YHWH), of Arnan (lion of perpetuity), of Obadiah (servant of YHWH), and of Shecaniah (dweller with YHWH).
3.22: The six descendants of Shecaniah (dweller with YHWH) were Shemaiah (heard by YHWH) and his sons: Hattush (assembled), Igal (He redeems), Bariah (fugitive), Neariah (servant of YHWH), and Shaphat (He has judged).
3.23: The sons of Neariah (servant of YHWH): Elioenai (unto YHWH are my eyes), Hizkiah (YHWH is my strength), and Azrikam (help against the enemy)—three in all.
3.24: The sons of Elioenai (unto YHWH are my eyes): Hodaviah (praise YHWH), Eliashib (God restores), Pelaiah (YHWH does wonders), Akkub (insidious), Johanan (YHWH has graced), Delaiah (YHWH has drawn), and Anani (my cloud)—seven in all.

4.1: The descendants of Judah (praised): Perez (breaking out), Hezron (surrounded by a wall), Carmi (my vineyard), Hur (hole), and Shobal (flowing).
4.2: Reaiah (YHWH has seen) son of Shobal (flowing) was the father of Jahath (He will snatch up), and Jahath was the father of Ahumai (brother of waters) and Lahad (oppression). These were the clans of the Zorathites.
4.3: These were the sons [These were of the father] of Etam (lair of wild beasts): Jezreel (God sows), Ishma (desolation), and Idbash (stout). And their sister was named Hazzelelponi (facing the shade).
4.4: Penuel (facing God) was the father (founder) of Gedor (wall), and Ezer (treasure) was the father of Hushah (haste). These were the descendants of Hur (hole), the firstborn of Ephrathah (place of fruitfulness) and the father of Bethlehem (house of bread).
4.5: Ashhur (black) the father of Tekoa (a stockade) had two wives, Helah (rust) and Naarah (maiden).
4.6: Naarah (maiden) bore to him Ahuzzam (possessor), Hepher (a well), Temeni (southern), and Haahashtari (I will observe the searching). These were the descendants of Naarah.
4.7: The sons of Helah (rust) were Zereth (splendor), Zohar [Izhar], Ethnan (hire of unchastity),
4.8: and Koz (thorn), who was the father of Anub (confederate) and Zobebah (the slow) and of the clans of Aharhel (a following host) son of Harum (exalted).
4.9: Now Jabez (sorrow) was more honorable than his brothers. His mother had named him Jabez (distress), saying, “Because I bore him in pain.
4.10: And Jabez (sorrow) called out to the God {Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God), “If only You would bless me and enlarge my territory! May Your hand be with me and keep me from harm, so that I will be free from pain. And God granted the request of Jabez.
4.11: Chelub (caged) the brother of Shuhah (wealth) was the father of Mehir (price), who was the father of Eshton (effeminate).
4.12: Eshton (effeminate) was the father of Beth-rapha (house of healing), of Paseah (limper), and of Tehinnah (supplication) the father of Ir-nahash (city of a serpent). These were the men of Recah (uttermost part).
4.13: The sons of Kenaz (hunter): Othniel (lion of God) and Seraiah (YHWH is ruler). The sons of Othniel: Hathath and Meonothai.
4.14: Meonothai (my habitations) was the father of Ophrah (fawn), and Seraiah (YHWH is ruler) was the father of Joab (YHWH is father), the father of those living in Ge-harashim (valley of craftsmen), which was given this name because its people were craftsmen.
4.15: The sons of Caleb (dog) son of Jephunneh (He will be facing): Iru (watch), Elah (An oak), and Naam (pleasantness). The son \sons/ of Elah: Kenaz (hunter).
4.16: The sons of Jehallelel (God is praised): Ziph (battlement), Ziphah, Tiria (fear), and Asarel (God holds).
4.17: The sons of Ezrah (help): Jether (abundance), Mered (rebellion), Epher (a calf), and Jalon (YHWH lodges). And Mered’s wife Bithiah gave birth \she gave birth/ to Miriam, Shammai (desolate), and Ishbah (he will praise) the father of Eshtemoa (fire of astonishment).
4.18: These were the children of Pharaoh’s (great house) daughter Bithiah. Mered also (rebellion) took a Judean (Judahite) wife, who gave birth to Jered (descended) the father of Gedor (wall), Heber (comrade) the father of Soco (bushy), and Jekuthiel (cleansing of God) the father of Zanoah (cast off).
4.19: The sons of Hodiah’s (my majesty is YHWH) wife, the sister of Naham (comfort), were the fathers of Keilah (fortress) the Garmite (bony) and of Eshtemoa (fire of astonishment) the Maacathite.
4.20: The sons of Shimon (desert): Amnon (faithful), Rinnah (shout), Ben-hanan (son of favor), and Tilon (gift). The descendants of Ishi (He saves me): Zoheth (releasing) and Ben-zoheth (son of Zoheth).
4.21: The sons of Shelah (a petition) son of Judah (praised): Er (awake) the father of Lecah (journey), Laadah (order) the father of Mareshah (crest of a hill) and the clans of the linen workers at Beth-ashbea (I shall make to swear),
4.22: Jokim (YHWH raises up), the men of Cozeba (falsehood), and Joash (given by the Lord) and Saraph (to burn), who ruled in Moab (of his father) and Jashubi-lehem (returner of bread). (These names are from ancient records.
4.23: These were the potters who lived at Netaim (among plants) and Gederah (wall). They lived there in the service of the king.
4.24: The descendants of Simeon (heard): Nemuel (day of God), Jamin (right hand), Jarib (he contends), Zerah (Zohar), and Shaul (desired).
4.25: The sons of Shaul: Shallum (retribution), Mibsam (sweet odor), and Mishma (a hearing).
4.26: The sons of Mishma (a hearing): Hammuel (heat of God), Zaccur (mindful), and Shimei (renowned).
4.27: Shimei (renowned) had sixteen sons and six daughters, but his brothers did not have many children, so their whole clan did not become as numerous as the sons of Judah (praised).
4.28: They lived in Beersheba (well of seven), Moladah (birth / race), Hazar-shual (jackal village),
4.31: Beth-marcaboth (house of the chariots), Hazar-susim (village of horses), Beth-biri (house of a creative one), and Shaaraim (double gate). These were their cities until the reign of David (beloved).
4.32: And their villages were Etam (lair of wild beasts), Ain (spring), Rimmon (pomegranate), Tochen (measurement), and Ashan (smoke)—five towns—
4.33: and all their surrounding villages as far as Baal [Baalath]. These were their settlements, and they kept a genealogical record:
4.34: Meshobab (restored / backsliding), Jamlech (He will reign), Joshah (YHWH makes equal) son of Amaziah (YHWH is mighty),
4.35: Joel (YHWH is God), Jehu (YHWH is He) son of Joshibiah (YHWH causes to dwell) (son of Seraiah (YHWH is ruler), son of Asiel (made by God)),
4.36: Elioenai (unto YHWH are my eyes), Jaakobah (toward Jacob), Jeshohaiah (YHWH humbles), Asaiah (made by YHWH), Adiel (ornament of God), Jesimiel (God will place), Benaiah (YHWH has built up),
4.37: and Ziza (shining) son of Shiphi (abundant) (son of Allon (oak), son of Jedaiah (praised of YHWH), son of Shimri (vigilant), son of Shemaiah (heard by YHWH)).
4.38: These men listed by name were the leaders of their clans. Their families increased greatly,
4.39: and they journeyed to the entrance of Gedor (wall), to the east side of the valley, in search of pasture for their flocks.
4.40: There they found rich, good pasture, and the land was spacious, peaceful, and quiet; for some Hamites (hot) had lived there formerly.
4.41: These who were noted by name came in the days of Hezekiah (YHWH is my strength) king of Judah (praised). They attacked the Hamites in their dwellings as well as the Meunites (habitations) who were there, devoting them to destruction {cherem} even to this day. Then they settled in their place, because there was pasture for their flocks.
4.42: And five hundred of these Simeonites (heard) led by Pelatiah (YHWH delivers), Neariah (servant of YHWH), Rephaiah (healed of YHWH), and Uzziel (my strength is God), the sons of Ishi (He saves me), went to Mount Seir (shaggy)
4.43: and struck down the remnant of the Amalekites (dwellers in a valley) who had escaped. And they have lived there to this day.

5.1: These were the sons of Reuben (behold a son) the firstborn of Israel (he wrestles with God). Though he was the firstborn, his birthright was given to the sons of Joseph (YHWH has added) son of Israel, because Reuben defiled his father’s bed. So he is not reckoned according to birthright.
5.2: And though Judah (praised) prevailed over his brothers and a ruler came from him, the birthright belonged to Joseph (YHWH has added).
5.3: The sons of Reuben (behold a son), the firstborn of Israel (he wrestles with God): Hanoch (dedicated), Pallu (distinguished), Hezron (surrounded by a wall), and Carmi (my vineyard).
5.4: The descendants of Joel (YHWH is God): Shemaiah (heard by YHWH) his son, Gog (mountain) his son, Shimei (renowned) his son,
5.5: Micah (who is like God) his son, Reaiah (YHWH has seen) his son, Baal (lord) his son,
5.6: and Beerah (well) his son, whom Tiglath-pileser (You will uncover the wonderful bond) king of Assyria (a step) carried into exile. Beerah was a leader of the Reubenites.
5.7: His relatives by their clans are recorded in their genealogy: Jeiel (God sweeps away) the chief, Zechariah (YHWH remembers),
5.8: and Bela (destruction) son of Azaz (strong), the son of Shema (hear), the son of Joel (YHWH is God). They settled in Aroer (ruins) and as far as Nebo (prophet) and Baal-meon (lord of the habitation).
5.9: They also settled in the east as far as the edge of the desert that extends to the Euphrates (fruitfulness) River, because their livestock had increased in the land of Gilead (rocky region).
5.10: During the days of Saul (desired) they waged war against the Hagrites (wanderer), who were defeated at their hands, and they occupied the dwellings of the Hagrites throughout the region east of Gilead (rocky region).
5.11: The descendants of Gad (troop) lived next to the Reubenites in the land of Bashan (fruitful), as far as Salecah (migration):
5.12: Joel was (YHWH is God) the chief, Shapham (bold) the second, then Jaanai (whom YHWH answers) and Shaphat (He has judged), who lived in Bashan (fruitful).
5.13: Their kinsmen by families were Michael (who is like God), Meshullam (friend), Sheba (seven), Jorai (YHWH has taught me), Jacan (troublesome), Zia (tumult), and Eber (the region beyond)—seven in all.
5.14: These were the sons of Abihail (my father is might) son of Huri (linen weaver), the son of Jaroah (new moon), the son of Gilead (rocky region), the son of Michael (who is like God), the son of Jeshishai (my old one), the son of Jahdo (His unity), the son of Buz (contempt).
5.15: Ahi (my brother) son of Abdiel (servant of God), the son of Guni, was head of their family.
5.16: They lived in Gilead (rocky region), in Bashan (fruitful) and its towns, and throughout the pasturelands of Sharon (a plain).
5.17: All of them were recorded in the genealogies during the reigns of Jotham (YHWH is perfect) king of Judah (praised) and Jeroboam (the people will contend) king of Israel (he wrestles with God).
5.18: The Reubenites (behold a son), the Gadites (a troop / fortune), and the half-tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget) had 44,760 warriors—valiant men who carried the shield and sword, drew the bow, and were trained for battle.
5.19: They waged war against the Hagrites (wanderer), as well as Jetur (enclosed), Naphish (refreshment), and Nodab (nobility).
5.20: And because they cried out to God {Elohim} in battle, they were helped against their enemies, and the Hagrites (wanderer) and all their allies were delivered into their hands. Because they put their trust in God, He answered their prayers.
5.21: They seized the livestock of the Hagrites—50,000 camels, 250,000 sheep, and 2,000 donkeys. They also took 100,000 captives,
5.22: and many others fell slain, because the battle belonged to God {Elohim}. And they occupied the land until the exile.
5.23: Now the people of the half-tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget) were numerous. They settled in the land from Bashan (fruitful) to Baal-hermon (that is (lord of destruction), Senir (snow mountain), also known as Mount Hermon \from Bashan to Baal-hermon and Senir and Mount Hermon./).
5.24: These were the heads of their families: Epher (a calf), Ishi (He saves me), Eliel (El is God), Azriel (my help is God), Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed), Hodaviah (praise YHWH), and Jahdiel (my unity is God). They were mighty men of valor, famous men, and heads of their families.
5.25: But they were unfaithful to the God {Elohim} of their fathers, and they prostituted themselves with the gods of the peoples of the land, whom God had destroyed before them.
5.26: So the God {Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God) stirred up the spirit {ruach} of Pul (distinguishing) king of Assyria (a step) (that is, Tiglath-pileser (You will uncover the wonderful bond) king of Assyria) to take the Reubenites, the Gadites (a troop / fortune), and the half-tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget) into exile. And he brought them to Halah (painful), Habor (joining), Hara (mountain land), and the river of Gozan (a cutting off), where they remain to this day.

6.1: The sons of Levi (joined to): Gershon (exile), Kohath (assembly), and Merari (bitter).
6.2: The sons of Kohath (assembly): Amram (exalted people), Izhar (shining oil), Hebron (association), and Uzziel (my strength is God).
6.3: The children of Amram (exalted people): Aaron (light bringer), Moses (drawn out), and Miriam (rebellion). The sons of Aaron: Nadab (generous), Abihu (he is my father), Eleazar (God has helped), and Ithamar (coast of palms).
6.4: Eleazar (God has helped) was the father of Phinehas (mouth of brass), Phinehas was the father of Abishua (my father is safety),
6.5: Abishua (my father is safety) was the father of Bukki (wasting), Bukki was the father of Uzzi (strong),
6.6: Uzzi (strong) was the father of Zerahiah (YHWH has risen), Zerahiah was the father of Meraioth (rebellious),
6.7: Meraioth (rebellious) was the father of Amariah (YHWH has promised), Amariah was the father of Ahitub (my brother is goodness),
6.8: Ahitub (my brother is goodness) was the father of Zadok (righteous), Zadok was the father of Ahimaaz (my brother is wrath),
6.9: Ahimaaz (my brother is wrath) was the father of Azariah (YHWH has helped), Azariah was the father of Johanan (YHWH has graced),
6.10: Johanan (YHWH has graced) was the father of Azariah (YHWH has helped), who served as priest in the temple that Solomon (peace) built in Jerusalem (city of peace),
6.11: Azariah (YHWH has helped) was the father of Amariah (YHWH has promised), Amariah was the father of Ahitub (my brother is goodness),
6.12: Ahitub (my brother is goodness) was the father of Zadok (righteous), Zadok was the father of Shallum (retribution),
6.13: Shallum (retribution) was the father of Hilkiah, Hilkiah (my portion is YHWH) was the father of Azariah (YHWH has helped),
6.14: Azariah (YHWH has helped) was the father of Seraiah (YHWH is ruler), and Seraiah was the father of Jehozadak (Jozadak).
6.15: Jehozadak (YHWH is righteous) went into captivity when the LORD {YHWH} sent Judah (praised) and Jerusalem (city of peace) into exile by the hand of Nebuchadnezzar (may Nebo protect the crown).
6.16: The sons of Levi (joined to): Gershom (Gershon), Kohath (assembly), and Merari (bitter).
6.17: These are the names of the sons of Gershom (foreigner): Libni (white) and Shimei (renowned).
6.18: The sons of Kohath (assembly): Amram (exalted people), Izhar (shining oil), Hebron (association), and Uzziel (my strength is God).
6.19: The sons of Merari (bitter): Mahli (sick) and Mushi (yielding). These are the clans of the Levites listed according to their fathers:
6.20: Of Gershom (foreigner): Libni (white) his son, Jahath (He will snatch up) his son, Zimmah (plotter) his son,
6.21: Joah (YHWH is brother) his son, Iddo (His witness) his son, Zerah (rising) his son, and Jeatherai (whom YHWH leads) his son.
6.22: The descendants of Kohath (assembly): Amminadab (my kinsman is noble) his son, Korah (bald) his son, Assir (prisoner) his son,
6.23: Elkanah (God has possessed) his son, Ebiasaph (Abiasaph) his son, Assir (prisoner) his son,
6.24: Tahath (station) his son, Uriel (God is my light) his son, Uzziah (my strength is YHWH) his son, and Shaul (desired) his son.
6.25: The descendants of Elkanah (God has possessed): Amasai (burdensome), Ahimoth (brother of death),
6.26: Elkanah (God has possessed) his son [Elkanah. The sons of Elkanah:], Zophai (honeycomb) his son, Nahath (rest) his son,
6.27: Eliab (Eliel) his son, Jeroham (showing pity) his son, and Elkanah (God has possessed) his son [and Samuel his son].
6.28: The sons of Samuel (his name is El): Joel his firstborn and Abijah (YHWH is my father) his second son.
6.29: The descendants of Merari (bitter): Mahli (sick), Libni (white) his son, Shimei (renowned) his son, Uzzah (strength) his son,
6.30: Shimea (fame) his son, Haggiah (festival of YHWH) his son, and Asaiah (made by YHWH) his son.
6.31: These are the men David (beloved) put in charge of the music in the house of the LORD {YHWH} after the ark rested there.
6.32: They ministered with song before the tabernacle, the Tent of Meeting, until Solomon (peace) built the house of the LORD {YHWH} in Jerusalem (city of peace). And they performed their duties according to the regulations given them.
6.33: These are the men who served, together with their sons. From the Kohathites: Heman (faithful) the singer, the son of Joel (YHWH is God), the son of Samuel (his name is El),
6.34: the son of Elkanah (God has possessed), the son of Jeroham (showing pity), the son of Eliel (Eliab), the son of Toah (lowly),
6.35: the son of Zuph (honeycomb), the son of Elkanah (God has possessed), the son of Mahath (grasping), the son of Amasai (burdensome),
6.36: the son of Elkanah (God has possessed), the son of Joel (YHWH is God), the son of Azariah (YHWH has helped), the son of Zephaniah (YHWH has treasured),
6.37: the son of Tahath (station), the son of Assir (prisoner), the son of Ebiasaph (my father has gathered), the son of Korah (bald),
6.38: the son of Izhar (shining oil), the son of Kohath (assembly), the son of Levi (joined to), the son of Israel (he wrestles with God).
6.39: Heman’s kinsman was Asaph (gatherer), who served at his right hand: Asaph the son of Berechiah (YHWH blesses), the son of Shimea (fame),
6.40: the son of Michael (who is like God), the son of Baaseiah [Maaseiah], the son of Malchijah (my king is YHWH),
6.41: the son of Ethni (my hire), the son of Zerah (rising), the son of Adaiah (YHWH has adorned Himself),
6.42: the son of Ethan (enduring), the son of Zimmah (plotter), the son of Shimei (renowned),
6.43: the son of Jahath (He will snatch up), the son of Gershom (foreigner), the son of Levi (joined to).
6.44: On the left were their kinsmen, the sons of Merari (bitter): Ethan (enduring) the son of Kishi (bow of YHWH), the son of Abdi (servant of YHWH), the son of Malluch (counselor),
6.45: the son of Hashabiah (YHWH has considered), the son of Amaziah (YHWH is mighty), the son of Hilkiah (my portion is YHWH),
6.46: the son of Amzi (my strength), the son of Bani (built), the son of Shemer (preserved),
6.47: the son of Mahli (sick), the son of Mushi (yielding), the son of Merari (bitter), the son of Levi (joined to).
6.48: Their fellow Levites were assigned to every kind of service of the tabernacle, the house of God {Elohim}.
6.49: But Aaron (light bringer) and his sons did all the work of the Most Holy Place (the Holy of Holies). They presented the offerings on the altar of burnt offering and on the altar of incense to make atonement for Israel (he wrestles with God), according to all that Moses (drawn out) the servant of God {Elohim} had commanded.
6.50: These were the descendants of Aaron (light bringer): Eleazar (God has helped) his son, Phinehas (mouth of brass) his son, Abishua (my father is safety) his son,
6.51: Bukki (wasting) his son, Uzzi (strong) his son, Zerahiah (YHWH has risen) his son,
6.52: Meraioth (rebellious) his son, Amariah (YHWH has promised) his son, Ahitub (my brother is goodness) his son,
6.53: Zadok (righteous) his son, and Ahimaaz (my brother is wrath) his son.
6.54: Now these were the territories assigned to the descendants of Aaron (light bringer) from the Kohathite clan for their settlements, because the first lot fell to them:
6.55: They were given Hebron (association) in the land of Judah (praised) and its surrounding pasturelands.
6.56: But the fields and villages around the city were given to Caleb (dog) son of Jephunneh (He will be facing).
6.57: So the descendants of Aaron (light bringer) were given Hebron (association) (a city of refuge), Libnah (pavement) , Jattir (plenty), Eshtemoa (fire of astonishment),
6.59: Ashan (Ain), Juttah, and Beth-shemesh (house of the sun), together with their pasturelands.
6.60: And from the tribe of Benjamin (son of the right hand) they were given Gibeon, Geba (hill), Alemeth (covering), and Anathoth (answers to prayer), together with their pasturelands. So they had thirteen cities in all among their families.
6.61: To the rest of the Kohathites (assembly), ten cities were allotted from the half-tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget).
6.62: The Gershomites (Gershonites) (foreigner), according to their clans, were allotted thirteen cities from the tribes of Issachar (there is recompense), Asher (happy), Naphtali (wrestling), and Manasseh (causing to forget) in Bashan (fruitful).
6.63: The Merarites (bitter), according to their clans, were allotted twelve cities from the tribes of Reuben (behold a son), Gad (troop), and Zebulun (exalted).
6.64: So the Israelites (God prevails) gave to the Levites these cities and their pasturelands.
6.65: They assigned by lot the cities named above from the tribes of Judah (praised), Simeon (heard), and Benjamin (son of the right hand).
6.66: And some of the clans of the Kohathites (assembly) were given cities from the tribe of Ephraim (doubly fruitful) for their territory:
6.67: They were given Shechem (shoulder) (a city of refuge ) with its pasturelands in the hill country of Ephraim (doubly fruitful), and Gezer (portion),
6.68: Jokmeam (gathered by the people), Beth-horon (house of hollowness),
6.69: Aijalon (field of deer), and Gath-rimmon (winepress of the pomegranate), together with their pasturelands.
6.70: And from the half-tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget) the rest of the clans of the Kohathites (assembly) were given Aner (boy) and Bileam (not of the people), together with their pasturelands.
6.71: The Gershomites received the following (foreigner): From the clan of the half-tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget) they were given Golan (their captivity) in Bashan (fruitful) and also Ashtaroth (star), together with their pasturelands.
6.72: From the tribe of Issachar (there is recompense) they were given Kedesh (holy place), Daberath (word),
6.73: Ramoth (heights), and Anem (fountains), together with their pasturelands.
6.74: From the tribe of Asher (happy) they were given Mashal (entreaty), Abdon (servile),
6.75: Hukok (appointed), and Rehob (broad place), together with their pasturelands.
6.76: And from the tribe of Naphtali (wrestling) they were given Kedesh (holy place) in Galilee (circuit), Hammon (warm springs), and Kiriathaim (two cities), together with their pasturelands.
6.77: The Merarites (bitter) (the rest of the Levites) received the following: From the tribe of Zebulun (exalted) they were given [Jokneam, Kartah,] Rimmono (pomegranate) and Tabor, together with their pasturelands.
6.78: From the tribe of Reuben (behold a son) east of the Jordan (flowing down) opposite Jericho (city of palms) they were given Bezer (gold ore) in the wilderness, Jahzah (trodden down) (Jahaz),
6.79: Kedemoth (eastern), and Mephaath (splendor / height), together with their pasturelands.
6.80: And from the tribe of Gad (troop) they were given Ramoth (heights) in Gilead (rocky region), Mahanaim (two camps),
6.81: Heshbon (stronghold), and Jazer (helped), together with their pasturelands.

7.1: The sons of Issachar (there is recompense): Tola (worm), Puah (Puvah), Jashub (he will return), and Shimron (watch-height)—four in all.
7.2: The sons of Tola (worm): Uzzi (strong), Rephaiah (healed of YHWH), Jeriel (taught by God), Jahmai (whom YHWH guards), Ibsam (pleasant), and Shemuel (his name is El), the heads of their families. In the days of David (beloved), 22,600 descendants of Tola were numbered in their genealogies as mighty men of valor.
7.3: The son \sons/ of Uzzi (strong): Izrahiah (YHWH will shine). The sons of Izrahiah: Michael (who is like God), Obadiah (servant of YHWH), Joel (YHWH is God), and Isshiah (YHWH will lend). All five of them were chiefs.
7.4: In addition to them, according to their genealogy, they had 36,000 troops for battle, for they had many wives and children.
7.5: Their kinsmen belonging to all the families of Issachar (there is recompense) who were mighty men of valor totaled 87,000, as listed in their genealogies.
7.6: The three sons of Benjamin (son of the right hand): Bela (destruction), Becher (young camel), and Jediael (God makes known).
7.7: The sons of Bela (destruction): Ezbon (hastening to discern), Uzzi (strong), Uzziel (my strength is God), Jerimoth (He is Most High), and Iri (belonging to a city), heads of their families—five in all. There were 22,034 mighty men of valor listed in their genealogies.
7.8: The sons of Becher (young camel): Zemirah (song), Joash (YHWH hastens), Eliezer (God is my helper), Elioenai (unto YHWH are my eyes), Omri (pupil of YHWH), Jeremoth (He is Most High), Abijah (YHWH is my father), Anathoth (answers to prayer), and Alemeth (covering); all these were Becher’s sons.
7.9: Their genealogies were recorded according to the heads of their families—20,200 mighty men of valor.
7.10: The son of Jediael (God makes known): Bilhan (their decrepitude). The sons of Bilhan: Jeush (He hurries to aid), Benjamin (son of the right hand), Ehud (I will give thanks), Chenaanah (trader), Zethan (olive), Tarshish (yellow jasper), and Ahishahar (brother of the dawn).
7.11: All these sons of Jediael (God makes known) were heads of their families, mighty men of valor; there were 17,200 fit for battle.
7.12: The Shuppites (serpents) and Huppites (protected) were descendants of Ir (belonging to a city), and the Hushites (who makes haste) were descendants of Aher (following).
7.13: The sons of Naphtali (wrestling): Jahziel (Jahzeel), Guni, Jezer (forming), and Shallum [Shillem] the descendants of Bilhah (troubled).
7.14: The descendants of Manasseh (causing to forget): Asriel (I shall be prince of God) through his Aramean (exalted) concubine. She also gave birth to Machir (sold) the father of Gilead (rocky region).
7.15: Machir (sold) took a wife from among the Huppites (protected) and Shuppites (serpents). The name of his sister was Maacah (oppression). Another descendant was named Zelophehad (first-born), who had only daughters.
7.16: Machir’s (sold) wife Maacah (oppression) gave birth to a son, and she named him Peresh (dung). His brother was named Sheresh (root), and his sons were Ulam (their leader) and Rekem (variegation).
7.17: The son of Ulam (their leader): Bedan (in judging). These were the sons of Gilead (rocky region) son of Machir (sold), the son of Manasseh (causing to forget).
7.18: His sister Hammolecheth (the queen) gave birth to Ishhod (man of majesty), Abiezer (my father is help), and Mahlah (disease).
7.19: And these were the sons of Shemida (wise): Ahian (brotherly), Shechem (shoulder), Likhi (learning), and Aniam (lament of people).
7.20: The descendants of Ephraim (doubly fruitful): Shuthelah (noise of breaking), Bered (hail) his son, Tahath (station) his son, Eleadah (God has adorned) his son, Tahath his son,
7.21: Zabad (he endows) his son, and Shuthelah (noise of breaking) his son. Ezer (treasure) and Elead (God has testified) were killed by the natives of Gath (winepress), because they went down to steal their livestock.
7.22: Their father Ephraim (doubly fruitful) mourned for many days, and his relatives came to comfort him.
7.23: And again he slept with his wife, and she conceived and gave birth to a son. So he named him Beriah (disaster), because tragedy had come upon his house.
7.24: His daughter was Sheerah (kinswoman), who built Lower and Upper Beth-horon (house of hollowness), as well as Uzzen-sheerah (portion of Sherah).
7.25: Additionally, Rephah (healing of the breath) was his son, Resheph his son, Telah (vigor) his son, Tahan (camp) his son,
7.26: Ladan (put in order) his son, Ammihud (my kinsman is majesty) his son, Elishama (my God has heard) his son,
7.27: Nun (Non) his son, and Joshua (YHWH saves) his son.
7.28: Their holdings and settlements included Bethel (house of God) and its villages, Naaran (juvenile) to the east, Gezer (portion) and its villages to the west, and Shechem (shoulder) and its villages as far as Ayyah (Ayyah) and its villages.
7.29: And along the borders of Manasseh (causing to forget) were Beth-shean (house of ease) (Beth-shan), Taanach (sandy), Megiddo (place of crowds), and Dor (generation), together with their villages. The descendants of Joseph (YHWH has added) son of Israel (he wrestles with God) lived in these towns.
7.30: The children of Asher (happy): Imnah (right hand), Ishvah (he will resemble), Ishvi (he resembles me), Beriah (with a friend), and their sister Serah (the prince breathed).
7.31: The sons of Beriah (with a friend): Heber (comrade), as well as Malchiel (my king is God), who was the father of Birzaith (in leanness).
7.32: Heber (comrade) was the father of Japhlet (he will deliver), Shomer (keeper), and Hotham (seal), and of their sister Shua (wealth).
7.33: The sons of Japhlet (he will deliver): Pasach (cut off), Bimhal (in circumcision), and Ashvath (sleek). These were Japhlet’s sons.
7.34: The sons of Shemer (preserved): Ahi (my brother), Rohgah (The sons of his brother Shemer: Rohgah), Hubbah (hidden), and Aram (exalted).
7.35: The sons of his brother Helem (Hotham): Zophah (a cruse), Imna (He will restrain), Shelesh (might), and Amal (labour).
7.36: The sons of Zophah (a cruse): Suah (sweeping), Harnepher (panting), Shual (jackal), Beri (a well), Imrah (bitterness),
7.37: Bezer (gold ore), Hod (splendor), Shamma (desert), Shilshah (strong), Ithran (Jether), and Beera (a well).
7.38: The sons of Jether (abundance): Jephunneh (He will be facing), Pispa (disappearance), and Ara (lion).
7.39: The sons of Ulla (yoke): Arah (traveller), Hanniel (favored of God), and Rizia (delight).
7.40: All these were the descendants of Asher (happy)—heads of their families, choice and mighty men of valor, and chiefs among the leaders. The number of men fit for battle, recorded in their genealogies, was 26,000.

8.1: Benjamin (son of the right hand) was the father of Bela (destruction) his firstborn, Ashbel (fire of Bel) the second, Aharah (a following brother) the third,
8.2: Nohah (rest) the fourth, and Rapha (tall) the fifth.
8.3: The sons of Bela (destruction): Addar (exceedingly glorious), Gera (a grain), Abihud (my father is majesty),
8.4: Abishua (my father is safety), Naaman (pleasantness), Ahoah (brother of rest),
8.5: Gera (a grain), Shephuphan (serpent), and Huram (noble born).
8.6: These were the descendants of Ehud (united) who were the heads of the families living in Geba (hill) and were exiled to Manahath (rest):
8.7: Naaman (pleasantness), Ahijah (brother of Yah), and Gera (a grain), who carried them into exile and (and Gera, that is Heglam,) who was the father of Uzza (strength) and Ahihud.
8.8: Shaharaim (double dawn) had sons in the country of Moab (of his father) after he had divorced his wives Hushim (who makes haste) and Baara (brutish).
8.9: His sons by his wife Hodesh (new moon): Jobab (a desert), Zibia (roe), Mesha (freedom), Malcam (great king),
8.10: Jeuz (counselor), Sachia (announcement), and Mirmah (fraud). These were his sons, heads of families.
8.11: He also had sons by Hushim (who makes haste): Abitub (my father is goodness) and Elpaal (God is maker).
8.12: The sons of Elpaal (God is maker): Eber (the region beyond), Misham (purification), Shemed (preserved) (who built Ono (vigorous) and Lod (travail) with its villages),
8.13: and Beriah (with a friend) and Shema (hear) (who were the heads of families living in Aijalon (field of deer) and who drove out the inhabitants of Gath (winepress))
8.14: Ahio (brotherly), Shashak (longing), Jeremoth (He is Most High),
8.15: Zebadiah (endowment of YHWH), Arad (a wild donkey), Eder (flock),
8.16: Michael (who is like God), Ishpah (He is bare), and Joha (YHWH gives life) were the sons of Beriah (with a friend).
8.17: Zebadiah (endowment of YHWH), Meshullam (friend), Hizki (strong), Heber (comrade),
8.18: Ishmerai (He keeps me), Izliah (whom God will preserve), and Jobab (a desert) were the sons of Elpaal (God is maker).
8.20: Elienai (unto God are my eyes), Zillethai (dark), Eliel (El is God),
8.21: Adaiah (YHWH has adorned Himself), Beraiah (YHWH has created), and Shimrath (guard) were the sons of Shimei (renowned).
8.22: Ishpan (he hides), Eber (the region beyond), Eliel (El is God),
8.24: Hananiah (God has favored), Elam (eternity), Anthothijah (YHWH's answer),
8.25: Iphdeiah (YHWH will redeem), and Penuel (facing God) were the sons of Shashak (longing).
8.26: Shamsherai (sunlike), Shehariah (dawning of YHWH), Athaliah (afflicted of YHWH),
8.27: Jaareshiah (whom YHWH nourishes), Elijah (my God is YHWH), and Zichri (memorable) were the sons of Jeroham (showing pity).
8.28: All these were heads of families, the chiefs according to their genealogies, and they lived in Jerusalem (city of peace).
8.29: Jeiel the father of Gibeon (hill city) lived [The father of Gibeon lived] in Gibeon. His wife’s name was Maacah (oppression),
8.30: and Abdon (servile) was his firstborn son, then Zur (rock), Kish (bent), Baal [Ner], Nadab (generous),
8.32: and Mikloth (staves), who was the father of Shimeah (Shimeam). They too lived alongside their relatives in Jerusalem (city of peace).
8.33: Ner (lamp) was the father of Kish (bent), Kish was the father of Saul (desired), and Saul was the father of Jonathan (YHWH has given), Malchishua (my king is wealth), Abinadab (my father is willing), and Esh-baal (Ish-bosheth).
8.34: The son of Jonathan (YHWH has given): Merib-baal (Mephibosheth), and Merib-baal was the father of Micah (Mica).
8.35: The sons of Micah (who is like God): Pithon (harmless), Melech (king), Tarea (Tahrea), and Ahaz (he has grasped).
8.36: Ahaz (he has grasped) was the father of Jehoaddah (YHWH has adorned), Jehoaddah (Jadah) was the father of Alemeth (covering), Azmaveth (strong unto death), and Zimri (my music), and Zimri was the father of Moza (fountain).
8.37: Moza (fountain) was the father of Binea. Raphah (Rephaiah) was his son, Eleasah (God has made) his son, and Azel (proximity) his son.
8.38: Azel (proximity) had six sons, and these were their names: Azrikam (help against the enemy), Bocheru (firstborn), Ishmael (God hears), Sheariah (valued by YHWH), Obadiah (servant of YHWH), and Hanan (he is merciful). All these were the sons of Azel.
8.39: The sons of his brother Eshek (oppressor): Ulam (their leader) was his firstborn, Jeush (He hurries to aid) second, and Eliphelet (God is deliverance) third.
8.40: The sons of Ulam (their leader) were mighty men of valor, archers, and they had many sons and grandsons—150 in all. All these were the descendants of Benjamin (son of the right hand).

9.1: So all Israel (he wrestles with God) was recorded in the genealogies written in the Book of the Kings of Israel. But Judah (praised) was exiled to Babylon (confusion by mixing) because of their unfaithfulness.
9.2: Now the first to resettle their own property in their cities were Israelites (God prevails), priests, Levites, and temple servants {Nethinim}.
9.3: Some of the descendants of Judah (praised), Benjamin (son of the right hand), Ephraim (doubly fruitful), and Manasseh (causing to forget) lived in Jerusalem (city of peace):
9.4: Uthai (helpful) son of Ammihud (my kinsman is majesty), the son of Omri (pupil of YHWH), the son of Imri (eloquent), the son (son of the right hand) of Bani, a descendant of Perez (breaking out) son of Judah (praised).
9.5: From the Shilonites: Asaiah (made by YHWH) the firstborn and his sons.
9.6: From the Zerahites (rising): Jeuel (God sweeps away) and 690 relatives.
9.7: From the Benjamites (son of the right hand): Sallu (weighed) son of Meshullam (friend), the son of Hodaviah (praise YHWH), the son of Hassenuah (bristling);
9.8: Ibneiah (YHWH builds) son of Jeroham (showing pity); Elah (An oak) son of Uzzi (strong), the son of Michri (worthy of price); Meshullam (friend) son of Shephatiah (YHWH has judged), the son of Reuel (friend of God), the son of Ibnijah (whom YHWH will build up);
9.9: and 956 of their relatives according to their genealogy. All these men were heads of their families.
9.10: From the priests: Jedaiah (YHWH has known), Jehoiarib (YHWH contends), and Jachin (He will establish);
9.11: Azariah (YHWH has helped) son of Hilkiah (my portion is YHWH), the son of Meshullam (friend), the son of Zadok (righteous), the son of Meraioth (rebellious), the son of Ahitub (my brother is goodness), the chief official of God’s temple;
9.12: Adaiah (YHWH has adorned Himself) son of Jeroham (showing pity), the son of Pashhur (freedom), the son of Malchijah (my king is YHWH); Maasai (work of the Lord) son of Adiel (ornament of God), the son of Jahzerah (whom God leads back), the son of Meshullam (friend), the son of Meshillemith (Meshillemoth), the son of Immer (he has said);
9.13: and 1,760 of their relatives, the heads of their families, able men for the work of the service of the house of God {Elohim}.
9.14: From the Levites: Shemaiah (heard by YHWH) son of Hasshub (considerate), the son of Azrikam (help against the enemy), the son of Hashabiah (YHWH has considered), a descendant of Merari (bitter);
9.15: Bakbakkar (searcher), Heresh (mute), Galal (influential), and Mattaniah (gift of YHWH) son of Mica (who is like God), the son of Zichri (memorable), the son of Asaph (gatherer);
9.16: Obadiah (servant of YHWH) son of Shemaiah (heard by YHWH), the son of Galal (influential), the son of Jeduthun (praising); and Berechiah (YHWH blesses) son of Asa (healer), the son of Elkanah (God has possessed), who lived in the villages of the Netophathites.
9.17: These were the gatekeepers: Shallum (retribution), Akkub (insidious), Talmon (oppressor), Ahiman (my brother is a gift), and their relatives. Shallum was their chief;
9.18: he was previously stationed at the King’s Gate on the east side. These were the gatekeepers from the camp of the Levites (joined to).
9.19: Shallum (retribution) son of Kore (crier), the son of Ebiasaph (Abiasaph), the son of Korah (bald), and his relatives from the Korahites (one of Korah) were assigned to guard the thresholds of the Tent, just as their fathers had been assigned to guard the entrance to the dwelling of the LORD {YHWH}.
9.20: In earlier times Phinehas (mouth of brass) son of Eleazar (God has helped) had been in charge of the gatekeepers, and the LORD {YHWH} was with him.
9.21: Zechariah (YHWH remembers) son of Meshelemiah (whom YHWH repays) was the gatekeeper at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting.
9.22: The number of those chosen to be gatekeepers at the thresholds was 212. They were registered by genealogy in their villages. David (beloved) and Samuel (his name is El) the seer had appointed them to their positions of trust.
9.23: So they and their descendants were assigned to guard the gates of the house of the LORD {YHWH}—the house called the Tent.
9.24: The gatekeepers were stationed on the four sides {ruachowt}: east, west, north, and south.
9.25: Their relatives came from their villages at fixed times to serve with them for seven-day periods.
9.26: But the four chief gatekeepers, who were Levites, were entrusted with the rooms and the treasuries of the house of God {Elohim}.
9.27: They would spend the night stationed around the house of God {Elohim}, because they were responsible for guarding it and opening it every morning.
9.28: Some of them were in charge of the articles used in worship, to count them whenever they were brought in or taken out.
9.29: Others were put in charge of the furnishings and other articles of the sanctuary, as well as the fine flour, wine, oil, frankincense, and spices.
9.30: And some of the sons of the priests mixed the spices.
9.31: A Levite named Mattithiah (gift of YHWH), the firstborn son of Shallum (retribution) the Korahite (one of Korah), was entrusted with baking the bread.
9.32: Some of their Kohathite relatives were responsible for preparing the rows of the showbread every Sabbath.
9.33: Those who were musicians, the heads of Levite families, stayed in the temple chambers and were exempt from other duties because they were on duty day and night.
9.34: All these were heads of Levite families, chiefs according to their genealogies, and they lived in Jerusalem (city of peace).
9.35: Jeiel (God sweeps away) the father (the founder) of Gibeon (hill city) lived in Gibeon. His wife’s name was Maacah (oppression).
9.36: Abdon (servile) was his firstborn son, then Zur (rock), Kish (bent), Baal (lord), Ner (lamp), Nadab (generous),
9.37: Gedor (wall), Ahio (brotherly), Zechariah (YHWH remembers), and Mikloth (staves).
9.38: Mikloth (staves) was the father of Shimeam (Shimeah). They too lived alongside their relatives in Jerusalem (city of peace).
9.39: Ner (lamp) was the father of Kish (bent), Kish was the father of Saul (desired), and Saul was the father of Jonathan (YHWH has given), Malchishua (my king is wealth), Abinadab (my father is willing), and Esh-baal (a man of Baal).
9.40: The son of Jonathan (YHWH has given): Merib-baal (Baal is my advocate), who was the father of Micah (who is like God).
9.41: The sons of Micah (who is like God): Pithon (harmless), Melech (king), Tahrea, and Ahaz [Pithon, Melech, and Tahrea].
9.42: Ahaz (he has grasped) was the father of Jarah (forested); Jarah was the father of Alemeth (covering), Azmaveth (strong unto death), and Zimri (my music); and Zimri was the father of Moza (fountain).
9.43: Moza (fountain) was the father of Binea. Rephaiah (Raphah) was his son, Elasah (God has made) his son, and Azel (proximity) his son.
9.44: And Azel (proximity) had six sons, and these were their names: Azrikam (help against the enemy), Bocheru (firstborn), Ishmael (God hears), Sheariah (valued by YHWH), Obadiah (servant of YHWH), and Hanan (he is merciful). These were the sons of Azel.

10.1: Now the Philistines (immigrants) fought against Israel (he wrestles with God), and the men of Israel fled before them, and many fell slain on Mount Gilboa (swollen heap).
10.2: The Philistines (immigrants) followed hard after Saul (desired) and his sons, and they killed Saul’s sons Jonathan (YHWH has given), Abinadab (my father is willing), and Malchishua (my king is wealth).
10.3: When the battle intensified against Saul (desired), the archers overtook him and wounded him.
10.4: Then Saul (desired) said to his armor-bearer, “Draw your sword and run me through with it, or these uncircumcised men will come and torture me! But his armor-bearer was terrified and refused to do it. So Saul took his own sword and fell on it.
10.5: When his armor-bearer saw that Saul (desired) was dead, he too fell on his own sword and died.
10.6: So Saul (desired) died together with his three sons and all his house.
10.7: When all the Israelites (God prevails) in the valley saw that the army had fled and that Saul (desired) and his sons had died, they abandoned their cities and ran away. So the Philistines (immigrants) came and occupied their cities.
10.8: The next day, when the Philistines (immigrants) came to strip the dead, they found Saul (desired) and his sons fallen on Mount Gilboa (swollen heap).
10.9: They stripped Saul, cut off his head, took his armor, and sent messengers throughout the land of the Philistines (immigrants) to proclaim the news in the temple of their idols and among their people.
10.10: They put his armor in the temple of their gods and hung his head in the temple of Dagon (a fish).
10.11: When all the people of Jabesh-gilead (dry) (rocky region) heard about everything the Philistines (immigrants) had done to Saul (desired),
10.12: all their men of valor set out and retrieved the bodies of Saul (desired) and his sons and brought them to Jabesh (dry). And they buried their bones under the oak (terebinth) in Jabesh and fasted seven days.
10.13: So Saul (desired) died for his unfaithfulness to the LORD {YHWH}, because he did not keep the word of the LORD and even consulted a medium for guidance,
10.14: and he failed to inquire of the LORD {YHWH}. So the LORD put him to death and turned the kingdom over to David (beloved) son of Jesse (I possess).

11.1: Then all Israel (he wrestles with God) came together to David (beloved) at Hebron (association) and said, “Here we are, your own flesh and blood.
11.2: Even in times past (For some time), while Saul (desired) was king, you were the one who led Israel out (he wrestles with God) and brought them back. And the LORD your God {YHWH Eloheka} said, ‘You will shepherd My people Israel, and you will be ruler over them.
11.3: So all the elders of Israel (he wrestles with God) came to the king at Hebron (association), where David (beloved) made a covenant with them before the LORD {YHWH}. And they anointed him king over Israel, according to the word of the LORD through Samuel (his name is El).
11.4: Then David (beloved) and all the Israelites (God prevails) marched to Jerusalem (city of peace) (that is, Jebus (threshing place)), where the Jebusites (Jerusalemites) inhabited the land.
11.5: The people of Jebus (threshing place) said to David (beloved), “You will never get in here. Nevertheless, David captured the fortress of Zion (that is, the City of David)
11.6: Now David (beloved) had said, “Whoever is the first to strike down a Jebusite (Jerusalemite) will become chief commander. And Joab (YHWH is father) son of Zeruiah (balsam) went up first, and he became the chief.
11.7: So David (beloved) took up residence in the fortress; that is why it was called the City of David.
11.8: He built up the city around it, from the supporting terraces \the Millo/ to the surrounding wall, while Joab (YHWH is father) restored the rest of the city.
11.9: And David (beloved) became greater and greater, for the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts was with him.
11.10: Now these were the chiefs of David’s (beloved) mighty men, who, together with all Israel (he wrestles with God), bolstered and strengthened his kingdom, according to the word of the LORD {YHWH} concerning Israel.
11.11: This is the list of David’s (beloved) mighty men: Jashobeam (the people will return) son of Hachmoni (wise) was chief of the officers [of the Three]; he wielded his spear against three hundred men, whom he killed at one time.
11.12: Next in command was Eleazar (God has helped) son of Dodo (Dodai) the Ahohite (brother of rest), one of the three mighty men.
11.13: He was with David (beloved) at Pas-dammim (boundary of blood) when the Philistines (immigrants) gathered there for battle. At a place with a field full of barley, the troops fled from the Philistines.
11.14: But Eleazar and David stationed themselves \But they stationed themselves/ in the middle of the field and defended it. They struck down the Philistines (immigrants), and the LORD {YHWH} brought about a great victory.
11.15: Three of the thirty chief men went down to David (beloved), to the rock at the cave of Adullam (justice of the people), while a company of Philistines (immigrants) was encamped in the Valley of Rephaim.
11.16: At that time David (beloved) was in the stronghold, and the garrison of the Philistines (immigrants) was at Bethlehem (house of bread).
11.17: David (beloved) longed for water and said, “Oh, that someone would get me a drink of water from the well (house of bread) near the gate of Bethlehem!
11.18: So the Three broke through the Philistine (immigrants) camp, drew water from the well (house of bread) at the gate of Bethlehem, and brought it back to David (beloved). But he refused to drink it; instead, he poured it out to the LORD {YHWH},
11.19: saying, “Far be it from me, my God {Elohay}, to do this! How can I drink the blood of these men who risked their lives? Because they had brought it at the risk of their lives, David refused to drink it. Such were the exploits of the three mighty men.
11.20: Now Abishai (my father is a gift), the brother of Joab (YHWH is father), was chief of the Three [the Thirty], and he wielded his spear against three hundred men, killed them, and won a name along with the Three.
11.21: He was doubly honored above the Three, and he became their commander, even though he was not included among the Three.
11.22: And Benaiah (YHWH has built up) son of Jehoiada (YHWH knows) was a man of valor from Kabzeel (God gathers), a man of many exploits. He struck down two champions of Moab (two sons of Ariel of Moab), and on a snowy day he went down into a pit and killed a lion.
11.23: He also struck down an Egyptian (double straits), a huge man five cubits tall <7 ft 6 in / 229 cm>. Although the Egyptian had a spear like a weaver’s beam in his hand, Benaiah went against him with a club, snatched the spear from his hand, and killed the Egyptian with his own spear.
11.24: These were the exploits of Benaiah (YHWH has built up) son of Jehoiada (YHWH knows), who won a name along with the three mighty men.
11.25: He was most honored among the Thirty, but he did not become one of the Three. And David (beloved) appointed him over his guard.
11.26: Now these were the mighty men: Asahel (God-made) the brother of Joab (YHWH is father), Elhanan (God has been gracious) son of Dodo (his beloved) of Bethlehem (house of bread),
11.27: Shammoth (desolations) the Harorite (Shammah the Harodite), Helez (he has saved) the Pelonite (a certain one),
11.28: Ira (watchful of a city) son of Ikkesh (twisted) the Tekoite, Abiezer (my father is help) the Anathothite,
11.29: Sibbecai (weaver) the Hushathite, Ilai (exalted) the Ahohite (brother of rest),
11.30: Maharai (impetuous) the Netophathite, Heled (transient) son of Baanah (in affliction) the Netophathite,
11.31: Ithai (Ittai) son of Ribai (pleader with YHWH) from Gibeah (hill) of the Benjamites (son of the right hand), Benaiah (YHWH has built up) the Pirathonite,
11.32: Hurai (Hiddai) from the brooks (from the ravines) of Gaash (quaking), Abiel (Abi-albon) the Arbathite,
11.33: Azmaveth (strong unto death) the Baharumite (Barhumite), Eliahba (God hides) the Shaalbonite,
11.34: the sons of Hashem (Jashen) the Gizonite (shearer), Jonathan (YHWH has given) son of Shagee (erring) the Hararite (mountain dweller),
11.35: Ahiam (mother's brother) son of Sachar (Sharar) the Hararite (mountain dweller), Eliphal (my God is supplication) son of Ur (flame),
11.36: Hepher (a well) the Mecherathite (he of the dug-out), Ahijah (brother of Yah) the Pelonite (a certain one),
11.37: Hezro (enclosed) the Carmelite, Naarai (handmaid) son of Ezbai (my humblings),
11.38: Joel (YHWH is God) the brother of Nathan (giver), Mibhar (choice) son of Hagri (wanderer),
11.39: Zelek (fissure) the Ammonite, Naharai (snorter) the Beerothite (my wells), the armor-bearer of Joab (YHWH is father) son of Zeruiah (balsam),
11.40: Ira (watchful of a city) the Ithrite (a remnant), Gareb (scabby) the Ithrite,
11.41: Uriah (YHWH is my light) the Hittite, Zabad (he endows) son of Ahlai (would that!),
11.42: Adina (slender) son of Shiza (splendor) the Reubenite, chief of the Reubenites, and the thirty with him,
11.43: Hanan (he is merciful) son of Maacah (oppression), Joshaphat (YHWH judges) the Mithnite (athlete),
11.44: Uzzia (strength of YHWH) the Ashterathite, Shama (obedient) and Jeiel (God sweeps away) the sons of Hotham (seal) the Aroerite,
11.45: Jediael (God makes known) son of Shimri (vigilant) and his brother Joha (YHWH gives life) the Tizite (you shall go forth),
11.46: Eliel (El is God) the Mahavite (propagators), Jeribai (my contentions) and Joshaviah (YHWH makes equal), the sons of Elnaam (God is delight), Ithmah (orphan) the Moabite (from father: what father?),
11.47: Eliel (El is God), Obed (serving), and Jaasiel (God is maker) the Mezobaite (the one set up ofYah).

12.1: Now these were the men who came to David (beloved) at Ziklag (winding), while he was still banished from the presence of Saul (desired) son of Kish (bent) (they were among the mighty men who helped him in battle;
12.2: they were archers using both the right and left hands to sling stones and shoot arrows; and they were Saul’s (desired) kinsmen from Benjamin (son of the right hand)):
12.3: Ahiezer (my brother is help) their chief and Joash (given by the Lord), who were the sons of Shemaah (the rumour) the Gibeathite (hilliness); Jeziel (the assembly of God) and Pelet (deliverance), the sons of Azmaveth (strong unto death); Beracah (blessing); Jehu (YHWH is He) the Anathothite;
12.4: Ishmaiah (YHWH will hear) the Gibeonite (little hill), a mighty man among the Thirty and a leader over the Thirty; Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed), Jahaziel (beheld of God), Johanan (YHWH has graced), and Jozabad (YHWH has endowed) the Gederathite (a wall);
12.5: Eluzai (God is my strength), Jerimoth (He is Most High), Bealiah (YHWH is master), Shemariah (kept by YHWH), and Shephatiah (YHWH has judged) the Haruphite (native of Haruph);
12.6: Elkanah (God has possessed), Isshiah (YHWH will lend), Azarel (God has helped), Joezer (YHWH is help), and Jashobeam (the people will return), who were Korahites (one of Korah);
12.7: and Joelah (may he avail) and Zebadiah (endowment of YHWH), the sons of Jeroham (showing pity) from Gedor (wall).
12.8: Some Gadites (a troop / fortune) defected to David (beloved) at his stronghold in the wilderness. They were mighty men of valor, trained for battle, experts with the shield and spear, whose faces were like the faces of lions and who were as swift as gazelles on the mountains:
12.9: Ezer (treasure) the chief, Obadiah (servant of YHWH) the second in command, Eliab (God is father) the third,
12.10: Mishmannah (fatness) the fourth, Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) the fifth,
12.11: Attai (opportune) the sixth, Eliel (El is God) the seventh,
12.12: Johanan (YHWH has graced) the eighth, Elzabad (God has given) the ninth,
12.13: Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) the tenth, and Machbanai (bond of the Lord) the eleventh.
12.14: These Gadites (troop) were army commanders, the least of whom was a match for a hundred, and the greatest for a thousand.
12.15: These are the ones who crossed the Jordan (flowing down) in the first month when it was overflowing all its banks, and they put to flight all those in the valleys, both to the east and to the west.
12.16: Other Benjamites (son of the right hand) and some men from Judah (praised) also came to David (beloved) in his stronghold.
12.17: And David went out (beloved) to meet them, saying, “If you? have come to me in peace to help me, my heart will be united with you?; but if you have come to betray me to my enemies when my hands are free of violence, may the God {Elohe} of our fathers see it and judge you.
12.18: Then the Spirit {Ruach} came upon Amasai (burdensome), the chief of the Thirty, and he said: “We are yours, O David (beloved)! We are with you, O son of Jesse (I possess)! Peace, peace to you, and peace to your helpers, for your God {Eloheka} helps you. So David received them and made them leaders of his troops.
12.19: Some from Manasseh (causing to forget) defected to David (beloved) when he went with the Philistines (immigrants) to fight against (desired) Saul. (They did not help the Philistines because the Philistine rulers consulted and sent David away, saying, “It will cost us our heads if he defects to his master Saul.
12.20: When David went to Ziklag (winding), these men of Manasseh (causing to forget) defected to him: Adnah (pleasure), Jozabad (YHWH has endowed), Jediael (God makes known), Michael (who is like God), Jozabad, Elihu (He is my God), and Zillethai (dark), chiefs of thousands in Manasseh.
12.21: They helped David (beloved) against the raiders, for they were all mighty men of valor and commanders in the army.
12.22: For at that time men came to David (beloved) day after day to help him, until he had a great army, like the army of God {Elohim}.
12.23: Now these are the numbers of men armed for battle who came to David (beloved) at Hebron (association) to turn Saul’s (desired) kingdom over to him, in accordance with the word of the LORD {YHWH}:
12.24: From Judah (praised): 6,800 armed troops bearing shields and spears.
12.25: From Simeon (heard): 7,100 mighty men of valor, ready for battle.
12.27: including Jehoiada (YHWH knows), leader of the house of Aaron (light bringer), with 3,700 men,
12.28: and Zadok (righteous), a mighty young man of valor, with 22 commanders from his own family.
12.29: From Benjamin (son of the right hand), the kinsmen of Saul (desired): 3,000, most of whom had remained loyal to the house of Saul up to that time.
12.30: From Ephraim (doubly fruitful): 20,800 mighty men of valor, famous among their own clans.
12.31: From the half-tribe of Manasseh (the half-tribe of Manasseh west of the Jordan): 18,000 designated by name to come and make David (beloved) king.
12.32: From Issachar (there is recompense), men who understood the times and knew what Israel (he wrestles with God) should do: 200 chiefs with all their kinsmen at their command.
12.33: From Zebulun (exalted): 50,000 fit for service, trained for battle with all kinds of weapons of war, who with one purpose were devoted to David.
12.34: From Naphtali (wrestling): 1,000 commanders, accompanied by 37,000 men with shield and spear.
12.35: From Dan (He has judged): 28,600 prepared for battle.
12.36: From Asher (happy): 40,000 fit for service, prepared for battle.
12.37: And from east of the Jordan (flowing down), from Reuben, Gad (a troop / fortune), and the half-tribe of Manasseh there (causing to forget): 120,000 armed with every kind of weapon of war.
12.38: All these men of war, arrayed for battle, came to Hebron (association) fully determined to make David (beloved) king over all Israel (he wrestles with God). And all the rest of the Israelites were of one mind to make David king.
12.39: They spent three days there eating and drinking with David (beloved), for their relatives had provided for them.
12.40: And their neighbors from as far away as Issachar (there is recompense), Zebulun (exalted), and Naphtali (wrestling) came bringing food on donkeys, camels, mules, and oxen—abundant supplies of flour, fig cakes and raisin cakes, wine and oil, oxen and sheep. Indeed, there was joy in Israel (he wrestles with God).

13.1: Then David (beloved) conferred with all his leaders, the commanders of thousands and of hundreds.
13.2: And he (beloved) said to the whole assembly of Israel (he wrestles with God), “If it seems good to you?, and if this is of the LORD our God {YHWH Elohenu}, let us send word far and wide to the rest of our brothers in all the land of Israel, and also to the priests and Levites in their cities and pasturelands, so that they may join us.
13.3: Then let us bring back the ark of our God {Elohenu}, for we did not inquire of Him (of it) in the days of Saul (desired).
13.4: And because this proposal seemed right to all the people, the whole assembly agreed to it.
13.5: So David (beloved) assembled all Israel (he wrestles with God), from the River Shihor \from the Shihor/ in Egypt (land of bondage) to Lebo-hamath (fortress), to bring the ark of God {Elohim} from Kiriath-jearim (city of forests).
13.6: David (beloved) and all Israel (he wrestles with God) went up to Baalah (mistress) of Judah (praised) (that is, Kiriath-jearim (city of forests)) to bring up from there the ark of God the LORD {Elohim YHWH}, who is enthroned between the cherubim—the ark that is called by the Name.
13.7: So they carried the ark of God {Elohim} from the house of Abinadab (my father is willing) on a new cart, with Uzzah (strength) and Ahio (brotherly) guiding the cart.
13.8: David (beloved) and all the Israelites (God prevails) were celebrating before God {Elohim} with all their might, with songs and on harps and lyres, with tambourines, cymbals, and trumpets.
13.9: When they came to the threshing floor of Chidon (Nacon), Uzzah (strength) reached out and took hold of the ark, because the oxen had stumbled.
13.10: And the anger of the LORD {YHWH} burned against Uzzah (strength), and He struck him down because he had put his hand on the ark. So he died there before God {Elohim}.
13.11: Then David (beloved) became angry because the LORD {YHWH} had burst forth against Uzzah (strength). So he named that place Perez-uzzah (breach of Uzza), as it is called to this day.
13.12: That day David (beloved) feared God {Elohim} and asked, “How can I ever bring the ark of God to me?
13.13: So he (beloved) did not move the ark with him to the City of David; instead, he took it aside to the house of Obed-edom (servant of Edom) the Gittite (belonging to Gath).
13.14: Thus the ark of God {Elohim} remained with the family of Obed-edom (servant of Edom) in his house for three months, and the LORD {YHWH} blessed his household and everything he owned.

14.1: Now Hiram (noble) king of Tyre (a rock) sent envoys to David (beloved), along with cedar logs, stonemasons, and carpenters, to build a palace for him.
14.2: And David (beloved) realized that the LORD {YHWH} had established him as king over Israel (he wrestles with God) and had highly exalted his kingdom for the sake of His people Israel.
14.3: And David (beloved) took more wives in Jerusalem (city of peace) and became the father of more sons and daughters.
14.4: These are the names of the children born to him in Jerusalem (city of peace): Shammua (Shimea), Shobab (rebellious), Nathan (giver), Solomon (peace),
14.5: Ibhar (YHWH chooses), Elishua (God is salvation), Elpelet (God is deliverance),
