14.7: Elishama (my God has heard), Beeliada (Eliada), and Eliphelet (God is deliverance).
14.8: When the Philistines (immigrants) heard that David (beloved) had been anointed king over all Israel (he wrestles with God), they all went in search of him; but David learned of this and went out to face them.
14.9: Now the Philistines (immigrants) had come and raided the Valley of Rephaim.
14.10: So David (beloved) inquired of God {Elohim}, “Should I go up against the Philistines (immigrants)? Will You deliver them into my hand? “Go, replied the LORD {YHWH}, “for I will deliver them into your hand.
14.11: So David and his men went up to Baal-perazim (The Lord Bursts Out), where he (beloved) defeated the Philistines and said, “Like a bursting flood, God {Elohim} has burst out against my enemies by my hand. So they called that place Baal-perazim.
14.12: There the Philistines abandoned their gods, and David (beloved) ordered that they be burned in the fire.
14.13: Once again the Philistines (immigrants) raided the valley.
14.14: So David (beloved) again inquired of God {Elohim}, who answered him, “Do not march up after them, but circle around them and attack them in front of the balsam trees (aspen trees).
14.15: As soon as you hear the sound of marching in the tops of the balsam trees, move out to battle, because this will mean that God {Elohim} has gone out before you to strike the camp of the Philistines (immigrants).
14.16: So David (beloved) did as God {Elohim} had commanded him, and they struck down the army of the Philistines (immigrants) all the way from Gibeon (hill city) to Gezer (portion).
14.17: And David’s (beloved) fame went out into every land, and the LORD {YHWH} caused all nations to fear him.

15.1: David constructed buildings for himself in the City of David (beloved), and he prepared a place for the ark of God {Elohim} and pitched a tent for it.
15.2: Then David (beloved) said, “No one but the Levites may carry the ark of God {Elohim}, because the LORD {YHWH} has chosen them to carry the ark of the LORD and to minister before Him forever.
15.3: And David (beloved) assembled all Israel (he wrestles with God) in Jerusalem (city of peace) to bring up the ark of the LORD {YHWH} to the place he had prepared for it.
15.4: Then he (beloved) gathered together the descendants of Aaron (light bringer) and the Levites:
15.5: From the Kohathites (assembly), Uriel (God is my light) the chief and 120 of his relatives;
15.6: from the Merarites (bitter), Asaiah (made by YHWH) the chief and 220 of his relatives;
15.7: from the Gershomites (Gershonites), Joel (YHWH is God) the chief and 130 of his relatives;
15.8: from the Elizaphanites (my God has protected), Shemaiah (heard by YHWH) the chief and 200 of his relatives;
15.9: from the Hebronites (association), Eliel (El is God) the chief and 80 of his relatives;
15.10: and from the Uzzielites (my strength is God), Amminadab (my kinsman is noble) the chief and 112 of his relatives.
15.11: David (beloved) summoned the priests Zadok (righteous) and Abiathar (my father is great) and the Levites Uriel (God is my light), Asaiah (made by YHWH), Joel (YHWH is God), Shemaiah (heard by YHWH), Eliel (El is God), and Amminadab (my kinsman is noble).
15.12: And he said to them, “You? are the heads of the Levitical families. You? and your? relatives must consecrate yourselves so that you? may bring the ark of the LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God), to the place I have prepared for it.
15.13: It was because you? Levites were not with us the first time that the LORD our God {YHWH Elohenu} burst forth in anger against us. For we did not consult Him about the proper order.
15.14: So the priests and Levites consecrated themselves to bring up the ark of the LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God).
15.15: And the Levites carried the ark of God {Elohim} on their shoulders with the poles, as Moses (drawn out) had commanded in accordance with the word of the LORD {YHWH}.
15.16: David also (beloved) told the leaders of the Levites to appoint their relatives as singers to lift up their voices with joy, accompanied by musical instruments—harps, lyres, and cymbals.
15.17: So the Levites appointed Heman (faithful) son of Joel (YHWH is God); from his brothers, Asaph (gatherer) son of Berechiah (YHWH blesses); from their brothers the Merarites (bitter), Ethan (enduring) son of Kushaiah (bow of YHWH);
15.18: and with them their brothers next in rank: Zechariah (YHWH remembers), Jaaziel (made bold by God), Shemiramoth (name of heights), Jehiel (God lives), Unni (afflicted), Eliab (God is father), Benaiah (YHWH has built up), Maaseiah (work of YHWH), Mattithiah (gift of YHWH), Eliphelehu (my God sets him apart), Mikneiah (possession of YHWH), and the gatekeepers Obed-edom (servant of Edom) and Jeiel [and Azaziah].
15.19: The musicians Heman (faithful), Asaph (gatherer), and Ethan (enduring) were to sound the bronze cymbals.
15.20: Zechariah (YHWH remembers), Aziel (Jaaziel), Shemiramoth (name of heights), Jehiel (God lives), Unni (afflicted), Eliab (God is father), Maaseiah (work of YHWH), and Benaiah (YHWH has built up) were to play the harps according to Alamoth (Alamoth).
15.21: And Mattithiah (gift of YHWH), Eliphelehu (my God sets him apart), Mikneiah (possession of YHWH), Obed-edom (servant of Edom), Jeiel (God sweeps away), and Azaziah (YHWH is mighty) were to lead the music with lyres according to Sheminith (Sheminith).
15.22: Chenaniah (YHWH establishes) the head Levite (burden) was the director of the music because he was highly skilled.
15.23: Berechiah (YHWH blesses) and Elkanah (God has possessed) were to be guardians \gatekeepers/ of the ark.
15.24: Shebaniah (increased by YHWH), Joshaphat (YHWH judges), Nethanel (given of God), Amasai (burdensome), Zechariah (YHWH remembers), Benaiah (YHWH has built up), and Eliezer (God is my helper)—the priests—were to blow the trumpets before the ark of God {Elohim}. Obed-edom (servant of Edom) and Jehiah (YHWH lives) were also to be guardians of the ark.
15.25: So David (beloved), the elders of Israel (he wrestles with God), and the commanders of thousands went with rejoicing to bring the ark of the covenant of the LORD {YHWH} from the house of Obed-edom (servant of Edom).
15.26: And because God {Elohim} helped the Levites who were carrying the ark of the covenant of the LORD {YHWH}, they sacrificed seven bulls and seven rams.
15.27: Now David (beloved) was dressed in a robe of fine linen, as were all the Levites who were carrying the ark, as well as the singers and Chenaniah (YHWH establishes), the director of music (burden) for the singers. David also wore a linen ephod.
15.28: So all Israel (he wrestles with God) brought up the ark of the covenant of the LORD {YHWH} with shouting, with the sounding of rams’ horns and trumpets, and with cymbals and the music of harps and lyres.
15.29: As the ark of the covenant of the LORD {YHWH} was entering the City of David (beloved), Saul’s (desired) daughter Michal (who is like God) looked down from a window and saw King David dancing and celebrating, and she despised him in her heart.

16.1: So they brought the ark of God {Elohim} and placed it inside the tent that David (beloved) had pitched for it. And they presented burnt offerings and peace offerings before God.
16.2: When David (beloved) had finished sacrificing the burnt offerings and peace offerings, he blessed the people in the name of the LORD {YHWH}.
16.3: Then he distributed to every man and woman of Israel (he wrestles with God) a loaf of bread, a date cake (a portion of meat), and a raisin cake.
16.4: David appointed some of the Levites to minister before the ark of the LORD {YHWH}, to celebrate, to give thanks, and to praise the LORD, the God {Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God).
16.5: Asaph (gatherer) was the chief, Zechariah (YHWH remembers) was second, then Jeiel (God sweeps away), Shemiramoth (name of heights), Jehiel (God lives), Mattithiah (gift of YHWH), Eliab (God is father), Benaiah (YHWH has built up), Obed-edom (servant of Edom), and Jeiel. They were to play the harps and lyres, while Asaph sounded the cymbals
16.6: and the priests Benaiah (YHWH has built up) and Jahaziel (beheld of God) blew the trumpets regularly before the ark of the covenant of God {Elohim}.
16.7: On that day David (beloved) first committed to Asaph (gatherer) and his associates this song of thanksgiving to the LORD {YHWH}:
16.8: “Give thanks to the LORD {YHWH}; call upon His name; make known His deeds among the nations.
16.9: Sing to Him, sing praises to Him; tell of all His wonders.
16.10: Glory in His holy name; let the hearts of those who seek the LORD {YHWH} rejoice.
16.11: Seek out the LORD {YHWH} and His strength; seek His face always.
16.12: Remember the wonders He has done, His marvels, and the judgments He has pronounced,
16.13: O offspring of His servant Israel (he wrestles with God), O sons of Jacob (he grasps the heel), His chosen ones.
16.14: He is the LORD our God {YHWH Elohenu}; His judgments carry throughout the earth.
16.15: Remember [He remembers] His covenant forever, the word He ordained for a thousand generations—
16.16: the covenant He made with Abraham (father of many), and the oath He swore to Isaac (he laughs).
16.17: He confirmed it to Jacob (he grasps the heel) as a decree, to Israel (he wrestles with God) as an everlasting covenant:
16.18: ‘I will give you the land of Canaan (lowland) as the portion of your? inheritance.
16.19: When they were few in number, few indeed, and strangers in the land,
16.20: they wandered from nation to nation, from one kingdom to another.
16.21: He let no man oppress them; He rebuked kings on their behalf:
16.22: ‘Do not touch My anointed ones! Do no harm to My prophets!
16.23: Sing to the LORD {YHWH}, all the earth. Proclaim His salvation day after day.
16.24: Declare His glory among the nations, His wonders among all peoples.
16.25: For great is the LORD {YHWH}, and greatly to be praised; He is to be feared above all gods.
16.26: For all the gods of the nations are idols, but it is the LORD {YHWH} who made the heavens.
16.27: Splendor and majesty are before Him; strength and joy fill His dwelling.
16.28: Ascribe to the LORD {YHWH}, O families of the nations, ascribe to the LORD glory and strength.
16.29: Ascribe to the LORD {YHWH} the glory due His name; bring an offering and come before Him. Worship the LORD in the splendor of His holiness;
16.30: tremble before Him, all the earth. The world is firmly established; it cannot be moved.
16.31: Let the heavens be glad, and the earth rejoice. Let them say among the nations, ‘The LORD {YHWH} reigns!
16.32: Let the sea resound, and all that fills it; let the fields exult, and all that is in them.
16.33: Then the trees of the forest will sing for joy before the LORD {YHWH}, for He is coming to judge the earth.
16.34: Give thanks to the LORD {YHWH}, for He is good; His loving devotion {chesed} endures forever.
16.35: Then cry out: ‘Save us, O God {Elohe} of our salvation; gather and deliver us from the nations, that we may give thanks to Your holy name, that we may glory in Your praise.
16.36: Blessed be the LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God), from everlasting to everlasting. Then all the people said, “Amen! and “Praise the LORD!
16.37: So David left Asaph (gatherer) and his associates there before the ark of the covenant of the LORD {YHWH}, to minister there regularly according to the daily requirements,
16.38: along with Obed-edom (servant of Edom) and his sixty-eight relatives. Obed-edom son of Jeduthun (praising), and also Hosah (refuge), were to be gatekeepers.
16.39: And David left Zadok (righteous) the priest and his fellow priests before the tabernacle of the LORD {YHWH} at the high place in Gibeon (hill city)
16.40: to regularly present burnt offerings to the LORD {YHWH} on the altar of burnt offerings, morning and evening, according to all that was written in the Law of the LORD, which He had commanded Israel to keep (he wrestles with God).
16.41: With them were Heman (faithful), Jeduthun (praising), and the rest of those chosen and designated by name to give thanks to the LORD {YHWH}, for “His loving devotion endures forever.
16.42: Heman (faithful) and Jeduthun (praising) had with them trumpets and cymbals for the music and instruments for the songs of God (instruments for the sacred music). And the sons of Jeduthun were stationed at the gate.
16.43: Then all the people departed for their homes, and David (beloved) returned home to bless his household.

17.1: After David (beloved) had settled into his palace, he said to Nathan (giver) the prophet, “Here I am, living in a house of cedar, while the ark of the covenant of the LORD {YHWH} is under a tent.
17.2: And Nathan (giver) replied to David (beloved), “Do all that is in your heart, for God {Elohim} is with you.
17.3: But that night the word of God {Elohim} came to Nathan (giver), saying,
17.4: “Go and tell My servant David (beloved) that this is what the LORD {YHWH} says: You are not the one to build Me a house in which to dwell.
17.5: For I have not dwelt in a house from the day I brought Israel up out of Egypt (Out of Egypt) until this day, but I have moved from tent to tent and dwelling to dwelling.
17.6: In all My journeys with all the Israelites (God prevails), have I ever asked any of the leaders (judges) I appointed to shepherd My people, ‘Why haven’t you? built Me a house of cedar?
17.7: Now then, you are to tell My servant David (beloved) that this is what the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts says: I took you from the pasture, from following the flock, to be the ruler over My people Israel (he wrestles with God).
17.8: I have been with you wherever you have gone, and I have cut off all your enemies from before you. Now I will make for you a name like that of the greatest in the land.
17.9: And I will provide a place for My people Israel (he wrestles with God) and will plant them so that they may dwell in a place of their own and be disturbed no more. No longer will the sons of wickedness oppress them as they did at the beginning
17.10: and have done since the day I appointed judges over My people Israel (he wrestles with God). And I will subdue all your enemies. Moreover, I declare to you that the LORD {YHWH} will build a house for you.
17.11: And when your days are fulfilled and you go to be with your fathers, I will raise up your descendant after you, one of your own sons, and I will establish his kingdom.
17.12: He will build a house for Me, and I will establish his throne forever.
17.13: I will be his Father, and he will be My son. And I will never remove My loving devotion from him as I removed it from your predecessor.
17.14: But I will set him over My house and My kingdom forever, and his throne will be established forever.
17.15: So Nathan (giver) relayed to David (beloved) all the words of this entire revelation.
17.16: Then King David (beloved) went in, sat before the LORD {YHWH}, and said, “Who am I, O LORD God {YHWH Elohim}, and what is my house, that You have brought me this far?
17.17: And as if this was a small thing in Your eyes, O God {Elohim}, You have spoken about the future of the house of Your servant and have regarded me as a man of great distinction (and have shown me future generations), O LORD {YHWH} God.
17.18: What more can David (beloved) say to You for honoring Your servant? For You know Your servant,
17.19: O LORD <2Sa 7:20>. For the sake of Your servant and according to Your own heart, You have accomplished this great thing and made known all these great promises.
17.20: O LORD {YHWH}, there is none like You, and there is no God {Elohim} but You, according to everything we have heard with our own ears.
17.21: And who is like Your people Israel (he wrestles with God)—the one nation on earth whom God {Elohim} went out to redeem as a people for Himself? You made a name for Yourself through great and awesome wonders by driving out nations from before Your people, whom You redeemed from Egypt (land of bondage).
17.22: For You have made Your people Israel (he wrestles with God) Your very own forever, and You, O LORD {YHWH}, have become their God {Elohim}.
17.23: And now, O LORD {YHWH}, let the word You have spoken concerning Your servant and his house be established forever. Do as You have promised,
17.24: so that Your name will be established and magnified forever when it is said, ‘The LORD {YHWH} of Hosts, the God {Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God), is God over Israel. And may the house of Your servant David (beloved) be established before You.
17.25: For You, my God {Elohay}, have revealed to Your servant that You will build a house for him. Therefore Your servant has found the courage to pray before You.
17.26: And now, O LORD {YHWH}, You are God {Elohim}! And You have promised this goodness to Your servant.
17.27: So now You have been pleased to bless the house of Your servant, that it may continue forever before You. For You, O LORD {YHWH}, have blessed it, and it will be blessed forever.

18.1: Some time later, David (beloved) defeated the Philistines (immigrants), subdued them, and took Gath (winepress) and its villages from the hand of the Philistines.
18.2: David also defeated the Moabites (of his father), and they became subject to David (beloved) and brought him tribute.
18.3: As far as Hamath (fortress), David (beloved) also defeated King Hadadezer (Hadad is help) of Zobah (station), who had marched out to establish his dominion \his hand/ along the Euphrates (fruitfulness) River.
18.4: David (beloved) captured from him a thousand chariots, seven thousand charioteers, and twenty thousand foot soldiers, and he hamstrung all the horses except a hundred he kept for the chariots.
18.5: When the Arameans (exalted) of Damascus (well-watered land) came to help King Hadadezer (Hadad is help) of Zobah (station), David (beloved) struck down twenty-two thousand of their men.
18.6: Then he (beloved) placed garrisons in Aram (exalted) of Damascus (well-watered land), and the Arameans became subject to David and brought him tribute. So the LORD {YHWH} made David victorious (the LORD saved David) wherever he went.
18.7: And David (beloved) took the gold shields that belonged to the officers of Hadadezer (Hadad is help) and brought them to Jerusalem (city of peace).
18.8: And from Tibhath (Tebah) and Cun (established), cities of Hadadezer (Hadad is help), David (beloved) took a large amount of bronze, with which Solomon (peace) made the bronze Sea, the pillars, and various bronze articles.
18.9: When King Tou (Toi) of Hamath (fortress) heard that David (beloved) had defeated the entire army of Hadadezer (Hadad is help) king of Zobah (station),
18.10: he sent his son Hadoram (Joram) to greet King David (beloved) and bless him for fighting and defeating Hadadezer (Hadad is help), who had been at war with Tou (wander). Hadoram brought all kinds of articles of gold and silver and bronze,
18.11: and King David (beloved) dedicated these to the LORD {YHWH}, along with the silver and gold he had carried off from all these nations—from Edom (red) and Moab (of his father), and from the Ammonites (tribal), Philistines (immigrants), and Amalekites (dwellers in a valley).
18.12: Moreover, Abishai (my father is a gift) son of Zeruiah (balsam) struck down eighteen thousand Edomites (red) in the Valley of Salt.
18.13: He placed garrisons in Edom (red), and all the Edomites were subject to David (beloved). So the LORD {YHWH} made David victorious wherever he went.
18.14: Thus David (beloved) reigned over all Israel (he wrestles with God) and administered justice and righteousness for all his people:
18.15: Joab (YHWH is father) son of Zeruiah (balsam) was over the army; Jehoshaphat (YHWH has judged) son of Ahilud (child's brother) was the recorder;
18.16: Zadok (righteous) son of Ahitub (my brother is goodness) and Ahimelech [Abimelech] son of Abiathar (my father is great) were priests; Shavsha (Shisha) was the scribe;
18.17: Benaiah (YHWH has built up) son of Jehoiada (YHWH knows) was over the Cherethites (executioners) and Pelethites (couriers); and David’s (beloved) sons were chief officials at the king’s side.

19.1: Some time later, Nahash (serpent) king of the Ammonites (tribal) died and was succeeded by his son.
19.2: And David (beloved) said, “I will show kindness to Hanun (gracious) son of Nahash (serpent), because his father showed kindness to me. So David sent messengers to console Hanun concerning his father. But when David’s servants arrived in the land of the Ammonites (tribal) to console him,
19.3: the princes of the Ammonites (tribal) said to Hanun (gracious), “Just because David has sent you comforters, do you really believe he is (beloved) showing respect for your father? Have not his servants come to you to explore the land, spy it out, and overthrow it?
19.4: So Hanun (gracious) took David’s (beloved) servants, shaved their beards, cut off their garments at the hips, and sent them away.
19.5: When someone came and told David (beloved) about his men, he sent messengers to meet them, since the men had been thoroughly humiliated. The king told them, “Stay in Jericho (city of palms) until your? beards have grown back, and then return.
19.6: When the Ammonites (tribal) realized that they had become a stench to David (beloved), Hanun (gracious) and the Ammonites sent a thousand talents of silver <37.7 t / 34.2 mt> to hire for themselves chariots and horsemen from Aram-naharaim (Aram of the two rivers), Aram-maacah (oppression), and Zobah (station).
19.7: So they hired for themselves thirty-two thousand chariots, as well as the king of Maacah (oppression) with his troops, who came and camped near Medeba (water of rest) while the Ammonites (tribal) were mustered from their cities and marched out for battle.
19.8: On hearing this, David (beloved) sent Joab (YHWH is father) and the entire army of mighty men.
19.9: The Ammonites (tribal) marched out and arrayed themselves for battle at the entrance to the city, while the kings who had come stayed by themselves in the open country.
19.10: When Joab (YHWH is father) saw the battle lines before him and behind him, he selected some of the best men of Israel (he wrestles with God) and arrayed them against the Arameans (exalted).
19.11: And he placed the rest of the troops under the command of his brother Abishai (my father is a gift), who arrayed them against the Ammonites (tribal).
19.12: “If the Arameans (exalted) are too strong for me, said Joab, “then you will come to my rescue. And if the Ammonites (tribal) are too strong for you, then I will come to your rescue.
19.13: Be strong and let us fight bravely for our people and for the cities of our God {Elohenu}. May the LORD {YHWH} do what is good in His sight.
19.14: So Joab (YHWH is father) and his troops advanced to fight the Arameans (exalted), who fled before him.
19.15: When the Ammonites (tribal) saw that the Arameans (exalted) had fled, they too fled before Joab’s brother Abishai (my father is a gift), and they entered the city. So Joab (YHWH is father) went back to Jerusalem (city of peace).
19.16: When the Arameans (exalted) saw that they had been defeated by Israel (he wrestles with God), they sent messengers to bring more Arameans from beyond the Euphrates \the River/, with Shophach (Shobach) the commander of Hadadezer’s (Hadad is help) army leading them.
19.17: When this was reported to David (beloved), he gathered all Israel (he wrestles with God), crossed the Jordan (flowing down), advanced toward the Arameans (exalted), and arrayed for battle against them. When David lined up to engage them in battle, they fought against him.
19.18: But the Arameans (exalted) fled before Israel (he wrestles with God), and David (beloved) killed seven thousand of their charioteers and forty thousand foot soldiers. He also killed Shophach (expansion) the commander of their army.
19.19: When Hadadezer’s (Hadad is help) subjects saw that they had been defeated by Israel (he wrestles with God), they made peace with David (beloved) and became subject to him. So the Arameans (exalted) were unwilling to help the Ammonites (tribal) anymore.

20.1: In the spring \At the turn of the year/, at the time when kings march out to war, Joab (YHWH is father) led out the army and ravaged the land of the Ammonites (tribal). He came to Rabbah (great) and besieged it, but David (beloved) remained in Jerusalem (city of peace). And Joab attacked Rabbah and demolished it.
20.2: Then David (beloved) took the crown from the head of their king (from the head of Milcom). It was found to weigh a talent of gold <75.4 lb / 34.2 kg> and was set with precious stones, and it was placed on David’s head. And David took a great amount of plunder from the city.
20.3: David brought out the people who were there and put them to work (cut them) with saws, iron picks, and axes. And he (beloved) did the same to all the Ammonite (tribal) cities. Then David and all his troops returned to Jerusalem (city of peace).
20.4: Some time later, war broke out with the Philistines (immigrants) at Gezer (portion). At that time Sibbecai (weaver) the Hushathite killed Sippai (Saph), a descendant of the Rephaim (the giants), and the Philistines were subdued.
20.5: Once again there was a battle with the Philistines (immigrants), and Elhanan (God has been gracious) son of Jair (forested) killed Lahmi (my bread) the brother of Goliath (splendor) the Gittite (belonging to Gath), the shaft of whose spear was like a weaver’s beam.
20.6: And there was also a battle at Gath (winepress), where there was a man of great stature with six fingers on each hand and six toes on each foot—twenty-four in all. He too was descended from Rapha,
20.7: and when he taunted Israel (he wrestles with God), Jonathan (YHWH has given) the son of David’s (beloved) brother Shimei (fame) killed him.
20.8: So these descendants of Rapha in Gath (winepress) fell at the hands of David (beloved) and his servants.

21.1: Then Satan (the adversary) rose up against Israel (he wrestles with God) and incited David (beloved) to take a census of Israel.
21.2: So David (beloved) said to Joab (YHWH is father) and the commanders of the troops, “Go and count the Israelites (God prevails) from (well of seven) Beersheba to Dan (He has judged) and bring me a report, so that I may know their number.
21.3: But Joab (YHWH is father) replied, “May the LORD {YHWH} multiply His troops a hundred times over. My lord the king, are they not all servants of my lord? Why does my lord want to do this? Why should he bring guilt on Israel (he wrestles with God)?
21.4: Nevertheless, the king’s word prevailed against Joab (YHWH is father). So Joab departed and traveled throughout Israel (he wrestles with God), and then he returned to Jerusalem (city of peace).
21.5: And Joab (YHWH is father) reported to David (beloved) the total number of the troops. In all Israel (he wrestles with God) there were 1,100,000 men who drew the sword, including 470,000 in Judah (praised).
21.6: But Joab did not include Levi (joined to) and Benjamin (son of the right hand) in the count, because the king’s command was detestable to him (YHWH is father).
21.7: This command was also evil in the sight of God {Elohim}; so He struck Israel (he wrestles with God).
21.8: Then David (beloved) said to God {Elohim}, “I have sinned greatly because I have done this thing. Now I beg You to take away the iniquity of Your servant, for I have acted very foolishly.
21.9: And the LORD {YHWH} instructed Gad (troop), David’s (beloved) seer,
21.10: “Go and tell David (beloved) that this is what the LORD {YHWH} says: ‘I am offering you three options. Choose one of them, and I will carry it out against you.
21.11: So Gad (troop) went and said to David (beloved), “This is what the LORD {YHWH} says: ‘You must choose
21.12: between three years of famine, three months of being swept away [of fleeing] before your enemies and overtaken by their swords, or three days of the sword of the LORD {YHWH}—days of plague upon the land, with the angel of the LORD ravaging every part of Israel (he wrestles with God). Now then, decide how I should reply to Him who sent me.
21.13: David (beloved) answered Gad (troop), “I am deeply distressed. Please, let me fall into the hand of the LORD {YHWH}, for His mercies are very great; but do not let me fall into the hands of men.
21.14: So the LORD {YHWH} sent a plague upon Israel (he wrestles with God), and seventy thousand men of Israel fell dead.
21.15: Then God {Elohim} sent an angel to destroy Jerusalem (city of peace), but as the angel was doing so, the LORD {YHWH} saw it and relented from the calamity, and He said to the angel who was destroying the people, “Enough! Withdraw your hand now! At that time the angel of the LORD was standing by the threshing floor of Ornan (Araunah) the Jebusite (Jerusalemite).
21.16: When David (beloved) lifted up his eyes and saw the angel of the LORD {YHWH} standing between heaven and earth, with a drawn sword in his hand stretched out over Jerusalem (city of peace), David and the elders, clothed in sackcloth, fell facedown.
21.17: And David (beloved) said to God {Elohim}, “Was it not I who gave the order to count the people? I (I, the shepherd,) am the one who has sinned and acted wickedly. But these sheep, what have they done? O LORD my God {YHWH Elohay}, please let Your hand fall upon me and my father’s house, but do not let this plague remain upon Your people.
21.18: Then the angel of the LORD {YHWH} ordered Gad (troop) to tell David (beloved) to go up and build an altar to the LORD on the threshing floor of Ornan (light was perpetuated) the Jebusite (Jerusalemite).
21.19: So David (beloved) went up at the word that Gad (troop) had spoken in the name of the LORD {YHWH}.
21.20: Now Ornan (light was perpetuated) was threshing wheat when he turned and saw the angel; and his four sons who were with him hid themselves.
21.21: David (beloved) came to Ornan (light was perpetuated), and when Ornan looked out and saw David, he left the threshing floor and bowed facedown before David.
21.22: Then David (beloved) said to Ornan (light was perpetuated), “Grant me the site of this threshing floor, that I may build an altar to the LORD {YHWH}. Sell it to me for the full price, so that the plague upon the people may be halted.
21.23: Ornan (light was perpetuated) said to David (beloved), “Take it! May my lord the king do whatever seems good to him. Look, I will give the oxen for the burnt offerings, the threshing sledges for the wood, and the wheat for the grain offering—I will give it all.
21.24: “No, replied (light was perpetuated) King David (beloved), “I insist on paying the full price, for I will not take for the LORD {YHWH} what belongs to you, nor will I offer burnt offerings that cost me nothing.
21.25: So David (beloved) paid Ornan (light was perpetuated) six hundred shekels of gold <15.1 lb / 6.8 kg> for the site.
21.26: And (beloved) there he built an altar to the LORD {YHWH} and offered burnt offerings and peace offerings. He called upon the LORD, who answered him with fire from heaven on the altar of burnt offering.
21.27: Then the LORD {YHWH} spoke to the angel, who put his sword back into its sheath.
21.28: At that time, when David (beloved) saw that the LORD {YHWH} had answered him at the threshing floor of Ornan (light was perpetuated) the Jebusite (Jerusalemite), he offered sacrifices there.
21.29: For the tabernacle of the LORD {YHWH} that Moses (drawn out) had made in the wilderness and the altar of burnt offering were presently at the high place in Gibeon (hill city),
21.30: but David (beloved) could not go before it to inquire of God {Elohim}, because he was afraid of the sword of the angel of the LORD {YHWH}.

22.1: Then David (beloved) said, “Here shall be the house of the LORD God {YHWH Elohim}, as well as the altar of burnt offering for Israel (he wrestles with God).
22.2: So David (beloved) gave orders to gather the foreigners in the land of Israel (he wrestles with God), from whom he appointed stonecutters to prepare finished stones for building the house of God {Elohim}.
22.3: David (beloved) provided a large quantity of iron to make the nails for the doors of the gateways and for the fittings, together with more bronze than could be weighed
22.4: and more cedar logs than could be counted; for the Sidonians and Tyrians had brought a large quantity of cedar logs to David (beloved).
22.5: And David (beloved) said, “My son Solomon (peace) is young and inexperienced, and the house to be built for the LORD {YHWH} must be exceedingly magnificent—famous and glorious throughout all lands. Therefore I must make preparations for it. So David made lavish preparations before his death.
22.6: Then David called for his son Solomon (peace) and instructed him to build a house for the LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God).
22.7: “My son, said David (beloved) to Solomon (peace), “it was in my heart to build a house for the Name of the LORD my God {YHWH Elohay},
22.8: but this word of the LORD {YHWH} came to me: ‘You have shed much blood and waged great wars. You are not to build a house for My Name because you have shed so much blood on the ground before Me.
22.9: But a son will be born to you who will be a man of rest. I will give him rest from all his enemies on every side; for his name will be Solomon (peace), and I will grant to Israel (he wrestles with God) peace and quiet during his reign.
22.10: He is the one who will build a house for My Name. He will be My son, and I will be his Father. And I will establish the throne of his kingdom over Israel (he wrestles with God) forever.
22.11: Now, my son, may the LORD {YHWH} be with you, and may you succeed in building the house of the LORD your God {YHWH Eloheka}, as He said you would.
22.12: Above all, may the LORD {YHWH} give you insight and understanding when He puts you in command over Israel (he wrestles with God), so that you may keep the Law of the LORD your God {YHWH Eloheka}.
22.13: Then you will succeed, if you carefully follow the statutes and ordinances that the LORD {YHWH} commanded Moses (drawn out) for Israel (he wrestles with God). Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid or discouraged.
22.14: Now behold, I have taken great pains to provide for the house of the LORD {YHWH}—100,000 talents of gold <3,770 t / 3,420 mt>, 1,000,000 talents of silver <37,700 t / 34,200 mt>, and bronze and iron too great to be weighed. I have also provided timber and stone, and you may add to them.
22.15: You also have many workers: stonecutters, masons, carpenters, and men skilled in every kind of work—
22.16: in gold and silver, bronze and iron—craftsmen beyond number. Now begin the work, and may the LORD {YHWH} be with you.
22.17: Then David (beloved) ordered all the leaders of Israel (he wrestles with God) to help his son Solomon (peace):
22.18: “Is not the LORD your? God {YHWH Eloheikem} with you?, and has He not granted you? rest on every side? For He has given the inhabitants of the land into my hand, and the land has been subdued before the LORD and His people.
22.19: Now set your? heart and soul to seek the LORD your? God {YHWH Eloheikem}. Begin building the sanctuary of the LORD God, so that you? may bring the ark of the covenant of the LORD and the holy articles of God into the temple that will be built for the Name of the LORD.

23.1: When David (beloved) was old and full of years, he installed his son Solomon (peace) as king over Israel (he wrestles with God).
23.2: Then he gathered all the leaders of Israel (he wrestles with God), as well as the priests and Levites.
23.3: The Levites thirty years of age or older were counted, and the total number of men was 38,000.
23.4: “Of these,” said David, “24,000 are to oversee the work of the house of the LORD {YHWH}, 6,000 are to be officers and judges,
23.5: 4,000 are to be gatekeepers, and 4,000 are to praise the LORD {YHWH} with the instruments I have made for giving praise.
23.6: Then David (beloved) divided the Levites into divisions according to the sons of Levi (joined to): Gershon (exile), Kohath (assembly), and Merari (bitter).
23.8: The sons of Ladan (put in order): Jehiel (Jehieli) the first, Zetham (olive), and Joel (YHWH is God)—three in all.
23.9: The sons of Shimei (renowned): Shelomoth (peaceful), Haziel (vision of God), and Haran (mountaineer)—three in all. These were the heads of the families of Ladan (put in order).
23.10: And the sons of Shimei (Shimei): Jahath (He will snatch up), Zina [Zizah], Jeush (He hurries to aid), and Beriah (with a friend). These were the sons of Shimei (renowned)—four in all.
23.11: Jahath (He will snatch up) was the first and Zizah (abundance) was the second; but Jeush (He hurries to aid) and Beriah (with a friend) did not have many sons, so they were counted as one family and received a single assignment.
23.12: The sons of Kohath (assembly): Amram (exalted people), Izhar (shining oil), Hebron (association), and Uzziel (my strength is God)—four in all.
23.13: The sons of Amram (exalted people): Aaron (light bringer) and Moses (drawn out). Aaron and his descendants were set apart forever to consecrate the most holy things, to burn incense before the LORD {YHWH}, to minister before Him, and to pronounce blessings in His name forever.
23.14: As for Moses (drawn out) the man of God {Elohim}, his sons were named among the tribe of Levi (joined to).
23.15: The sons of Moses (drawn out): Gershom (foreigner) and Eliezer (God is my helper).
23.16: The descendants of Gershom (foreigner): Shebuel (captive of God) was the first.
23.17: The descendants of Eliezer (God is my helper): Rehabiah (YHWH has enlarged) was the first. Eliezer did not have any other sons, but the sons of Rehabiah were very numerous.
23.18: The sons of Izhar (shining oil): Shelomith (peaceful) was the first.
23.19: The sons of Hebron (association): Jeriah (taught by Yah) was the first, Amariah (YHWH has promised) the second, Jahaziel (beheld of God) the third, and Jekameam (let the people rise) the fourth.
23.20: The sons of Uzziel (my strength is God): Micah (who is like God) was the first and Isshiah (YHWH will lend) the second.
23.21: The sons of Merari (bitter): Mahli (sick) and Mushi (yielding). The sons of Mahli: Eleazar (God has helped) and Kish (bent).
23.22: Eleazar (God has helped) died without having any sons; he had only daughters. Their cousins, the sons of Kish (bent), married them.
23.23: The sons of Mushi (yielding): Mahli (sick), Eder (a flock), and Jeremoth (Jerimoth) three in all.
23.24: These were the descendants of Levi (joined to) by their families—the heads of families, registered individually by name—those twenty years of age or older who worked in the service of the house of the LORD {YHWH}.
23.25: For David (beloved) had said, “The LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God), has given rest to His people and has come to dwell in Jerusalem (city of peace) forever.
23.26: So now the Levites no longer need to carry the tabernacle or any of the articles for its service.
23.27: For according to the final instructions of David (beloved), the Levites (joined to) twenty years of age or older were counted,
23.28: but their duty was to assist the descendants of Aaron (light bringer) with the service of the house of the LORD {YHWH}, being responsible for the courts and chambers, the purification of all the holy things, and the work of the service of the house of God {Elohim},
23.29: as well as for the rows of the showbread, the fine flour for the grain offering, the wafers of unleavened bread, the baking, the mixing, and all measurements of quantity and size.
23.30: They were also to stand every morning to give thanks and praise to the LORD {YHWH}, and likewise in the evening.
23.31: Whenever burnt offerings were presented to the LORD {YHWH} on the Sabbaths, New Moons, and appointed feasts, they were to serve regularly before the LORD in the numbers prescribed for them.
23.32: So the Levites were to carry out the responsibilities for the Tent of Meeting and the Holy Place, and, under their brothers the descendants of Aaron (light bringer), the service of the house of the LORD {YHWH}.

24.1: These were the divisions of the descendants of Aaron (light bringer). The sons of Aaron were Nadab (generous), Abihu (he is my father), Eleazar (God has helped), and Ithamar (coast of palms).
24.2: But Nadab (generous) and Abihu (he is my father) died before their father did, and they had no sons; so Eleazar (God has helped) and Ithamar (coast of palms) served as priests.
24.3: With the help of Eleazar’s (God has helped) descendant Zadok (righteous) and Ithamar’s (coast of palms) descendant Ahimelech (my brother is king), David (beloved) divided them according to the offices of their service.
24.4: Since more leaders were found among Eleazar’s (God has helped) descendants than those of Ithamar (coast of palms), they were divided accordingly. There were sixteen heads of families from the descendants of Eleazar and eight from the descendants of Ithamar.
24.5: Thus they were divided by lot, for there were officers of the sanctuary and officers of God {Elohim} among both Eleazar’s (God has helped) and Ithamar’s (coast of palms) descendants.
24.6: The scribe, Shemaiah (heard by YHWH) son of Nethanel (given of God), a Levite, recorded their names in the presence of the king and of the officers: Zadok (righteous) the priest, Ahimelech (my brother is king) son of Abiathar (my father is great), and the heads of families of the priests and the Levites—one family being taken from Eleazar (God has helped), and then one from Ithamar (coast of palms).
24.7: The first lot fell to Jehoiarib (YHWH contends), the second to Jedaiah (YHWH has known),
24.8: the third to Harim (dedicated), the fourth to Seorim (barley),
24.9: the fifth to Malchijah (my king is YHWH), the sixth to Mijamin (from the right hand),
24.10: the seventh to Hakkoz (thorn), the eighth to Abijah (YHWH is my father),
24.11: the ninth to Jeshua (he is saved), the tenth to Shecaniah (dweller with YHWH),
24.12: the eleventh to Eliashib (God restores), the twelfth to Jakim (He will raise),
24.13: the thirteenth to Huppah (canopy), the fourteenth to Jeshebeab (dwelling of the father),
24.14: the fifteenth to Bilgah (cheerfulness), the sixteenth to Immer (he has said),
24.15: the seventeenth to Hezir (swine), the eighteenth to Happizzez (to break),
24.16: the nineteenth to Pethahiah (freed by YHWH), the twentieth to Jehezkel (God strengthens),
24.17: the twenty-first to Jachin (He will establish), the twenty-second to Gamul (weaned),
24.18: the twenty-third to Delaiah (YHWH has drawn), and the twenty-fourth to Maaziah (consolation of YHWH).
24.19: This was their appointed order for service when they entered the house of the LORD {YHWH}, according to the regulations prescribed for them by their forefather Aaron (light bringer), as the LORD, the God {Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God), had commanded him.
24.20: Now these were the rest of the descendants of Levi (joined to): From the sons of Amram (exalted people): Shubael (Shebuel); from the sons of Shubael (captive of God): Jehdeiah (YHWH is unity).
24.21: As for Rehabiah (YHWH has enlarged), from his sons: The first was Isshiah (Jeshaiah).
24.22: From the Izharites (oil): Shelomoth (Shelomith); from the sons of Shelomoth (peaceful): Jahath (He will snatch up).
24.23: From the sons of Hebron: Jeriah was the first \From the sons: Jeriah/, Amariah (YHWH has promised) the second, Jahaziel (beheld of God) the third, and Jekameam (let the people rise) the fourth.
24.24: From the sons of Uzziel (my strength is God): Micah (who is like God); from the sons of Micah: Shamir (guardian).
24.25: The brother of Micah (who is like God): Isshiah (YHWH will lend); from the sons of Isshiah: Zechariah (YHWH remembers).
24.26: The sons of Merari (bitter): Mahli (sick) and Mushi (yielding). The son of Jaaziah (made bold by YHWH): Beno.
24.27: The descendants of Merari (bitter) from Jaaziah (made bold by YHWH): Beno, Shoham (onyx), Zaccur (mindful), and Ibri (Hebrew).
24.28: From Mahli (sick): Eleazar (God has helped), who had no sons.
24.29: From Kish (bent): Jerahmeel (may God have pity) the son of Kish.
24.30: And the sons of Mushi (yielding): Mahli (sick), Eder (a flock), and Jerimoth (Jeremoth). These were the sons of the Levites, according to their families.
24.31: As their brothers the descendants of Aaron did (light bringer), they also cast lots in the presence of King David (beloved) and of Zadok (righteous), Ahimelech (my brother is king), and the heads of the families of the priests and Levites—the family heads and their younger brothers alike.

25.1: Additionally, David (beloved) and the commanders of the army set apart some of the sons of Asaph (gatherer), Heman (faithful), and Jeduthun (praising) to prophesy with the accompaniment of lyres, harps, and cymbals. The following is the list of the men who performed this service:
25.2: From the sons of Asaph (gatherer): Zaccur (mindful), Joseph (YHWH has added), Nethaniah (given of YHWH), and Asarelah (God holds). These sons of Asaph were under the direction of Asaph, who prophesied under the direction of the king.
25.3: (praising) From the sons of Jeduthun: Gedaliah (YHWH is great), Zeri (built), Jeshaiah, Shimei, Hashabiah (YHWH has considered), and Mattithiah (gift of YHWH)—six in all—under the direction of their father Jeduthun, who prophesied with the harp, giving thanks and praise to the LORD {YHWH}.
25.4: (faithful) From the sons of Heman: Bukkiah (YHWH has emptied), Mattaniah (gift of YHWH), Uzziel (my strength is God), Shebuel (Shubael), Jerimoth (Jeremoth), Hananiah (God has favored), Hanani (gracious), Eliathah (God has come), Giddalti (I make great), Romamti-ezer (I have exalted the Helper), Joshbekashah (seated in hardness), Mallothi (I have uttered), Hothir (abundance), and Mahazioth (visions).
25.5: All these sons of Heman (faithful) the king’s seer were given him through the promises of God {Elohim} to exalt him, for God had given Heman fourteen sons and three daughters.
25.6: All these were under the direction of their fathers for the music of the house of the LORD {YHWH} with cymbals, harps, and lyres, for the service of the house of God {Elohim}. Asaph (gatherer), Jeduthun (praising), and Heman (faithful) were under the direction of the king.
25.7: Together with their relatives, who were all trained and skillful in the songs of the LORD {YHWH}, they numbered 288.
25.8: They cast lots for their duties, young and old alike, teacher as well as pupil.
25.9: The first lot, which was for Asaph (gatherer), fell to Joseph, his sons, and his brothers—12 in all; the second to Gedaliah (YHWH is great), his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;
25.10: the third to Zaccur (mindful), his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;
25.11: the fourth to Izri (Zeri), his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;
25.12: the fifth to Nethaniah (given of YHWH), his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;
25.13: the sixth to Bukkiah (YHWH has emptied), his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;
25.14: the seventh to Jesarelah (Asarelah), his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;
25.15: the eighth to Jeshaiah (YHWH has saved), his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;
25.16: the ninth to Mattaniah (gift of YHWH), his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;
25.17: the tenth to Shimei (renowned), his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;
25.18: the eleventh to Azarel (Uzziel), his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;
25.19: the twelfth to Hashabiah (YHWH has considered), his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;
25.20: the thirteenth to Shubael (captive of God), his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;
25.21: the fourteenth to Mattithiah (gift of YHWH), his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;
25.22: the fifteenth to Jeremoth (Jerimoth), his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;
25.23: the sixteenth to Hananiah (God has favored), his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;
25.24: the seventeenth to Joshbekashah (seated in hardness), his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;
25.25: the eighteenth to Hanani (gracious), his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;
25.26: the nineteenth to Mallothi (I have uttered), his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;
25.27: the twentieth to Eliathah (God has come), his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;
25.28: the twenty-first to Hothir (abundance), his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;
25.29: the twenty-second to Giddalti (I make great), his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;
25.30: the twenty-third to Mahazioth (visions), his sons, and his brothers—12 in all;
25.31: and the twenty-fourth to Romamti-ezer (I have exalted the Helper), his sons, and his brothers—12 in all.

26.1: These were the divisions of the gatekeepers: From the Korahites (one of Korah): Meshelemiah (whom YHWH repays) son of Kore (crier), one of the sons of Asaph (gatherer).
26.2: Meshelemiah (whom YHWH repays) had sons: Zechariah (YHWH remembers) the firstborn, Jediael (God makes known) the second, Zebadiah (endowment of YHWH) the third, Jathniel (God hires me) the fourth,
26.3: Elam (eternity) the fifth, Jehohanan (YHWH has graced) the sixth, and Eliehoenai (unto YHWH are my eyes) the seventh.
26.4: And Obed-edom (servant of Edom) also had sons: Shemaiah (heard by YHWH) the firstborn, Jehozabad (YHWH has endowed) the second, Joah (YHWH is brother) the third, Sachar (wages) the fourth, Nethanel (given of God) the fifth,
26.5: Ammiel (my kinsman is God) the sixth, Issachar (there is recompense) the seventh, and Peullethai (work) the eighth. For God {Elohim} had blessed Obed-edom.
26.6: Also to his son Shemaiah (heard by YHWH) were born sons who ruled over their families because they were strong, capable men.
26.7: Shemaiah’s (heard by YHWH) sons were Othni (lion of YHWH), Rephael (healed of God), Obed (serving), and Elzabad (God has given); his brothers were Elihu (He is my God) and Semachiah (YHWH has sustained), also capable men.
26.8: All these were descendants of Obed-edom (servant of Edom); they and their sons and brothers were capable men with strength to do the work—62 in all from Obed-edom.
26.9: Meshelemiah (whom YHWH repays) also had sons and brothers who were capable men—18 in all.
26.10: Hosah (refuge) the Merarite (bitter) also had sons: Shimri (vigilant) the first (although he was not the firstborn, his father had appointed him as the first),
26.11: Hilkiah (my portion is YHWH) the second, Tebaliah (purified) the third, and Zechariah (YHWH remembers) the fourth. The sons and brothers of Hosah (refuge) numbered 13 in all.
26.12: These divisions of the gatekeepers, through their chief men, had duties for ministering in the house of the LORD {YHWH}, just as their relatives did.
26.13: They cast lots for each gate, according to their families, young and old alike.
26.14: The lot for the East Gate fell to Shelemiah (Meshelemiah). Then lots were cast for his son Zechariah (YHWH remembers), a wise counselor, and the lot for the North Gate fell to him.
26.15: The lot for the South Gate fell to Obed-edom (servant of Edom), and the lot for the storehouses to his sons.
26.16: The lots for the West Gate and the Shallecheth (overthrow) Gate on the ascending highway (on the upper road) fell to Shuppim (serpents) and Hosah (refuge). There were guards stationed at every watch.
26.17: Each day there were six Levites on the east, four on the north, four on the south, and two pairs at the storehouse.
26.18: As for the court {parbar} on the west, there were four at the highway and two at the court (open apartment).
26.19: These were the divisions of the gatekeepers who were descendants of Korah (one of Korah) and Merari (bitter).
26.20: Now their fellow Levites were [As for the Levites, Ahijah was] in charge of the treasuries of the house of God {Elohim} and the treasuries of the dedicated things.
26.21: From the descendants of Ladan (put in order), who were Gershonites through Ladan (Libni) and heads of the families of Ladan the Gershonite, were Jehieli (Jehiel),
26.22: the sons of Jehieli (God shall save alive), Zetham (olive), and his brother Joel (YHWH is God). They were in charge of the treasuries of the house of the LORD {YHWH}.
26.23: From the Amramites, the Izharites (oil), the Hebronites, and the Uzzielites:
26.24: Shebuel (captive of God), a descendant of Gershom (foreigner) son of Moses (drawn out), was the officer in charge of the treasuries.
26.25: His relatives through Eliezer included (God is my helper) Rehabiah (YHWH has enlarged) his son, Jeshaiah (Isshiah) his son, Joram (YHWH is exalted) his son, Zichri (memorable) his son, and Shelomith (peaceful) his son.
26.26: This Shelomith (peaceful) and his brothers were in charge of all the treasuries for the things dedicated by King David (beloved), by the heads of families who were the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, and by the army commanders.
26.27: They had dedicated some of the plunder from their battles to the repair of the house of the LORD {YHWH}.
26.28: Everything that had been dedicated by Samuel (his name is El) the seer, Saul (desired) son of Kish (bent), Abner (my father is a lamp) son of Ner (lamp), and Joab (YHWH is father) son of Zeruiah (balsam), along with everything else that was dedicated, was under the care of Shelomith (peaceful) and his brothers.
26.29: From the Izharites (oil), Chenaniah (YHWH establishes) and his sons had the outside duties (the duties outside the temple) as officers and judges over Israel (he wrestles with God).
26.30: From the Hebronites, Hashabiah (YHWH has considered) and his relatives, 1,700 capable men, had charge of the affairs of Israel (he wrestles with God) west of the Jordan (flowing down) for all the work of the LORD {YHWH} and for the service of the king.
26.31: As for the Hebronites, Jerijah (Jeriah) was the chief of the Hebronites, according to the genealogies of his ancestors. In the fortieth year of David’s (beloved) reign the records were searched, and strong, capable men were found among the Hebronites at Jazer (helped) in Gilead (rocky region).
26.32: Among Jerijah’s relatives there were 2,700 capable men who were heads of families. King David (beloved) appointed them over the Reubenites, the Gadites (a troop / fortune), and the half-tribe of Manasseh for every matter pertaining to God {Elohim} and for the affairs of the king.

27.1: This is the list of the Israelites (God prevails)—the heads of families, the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, and their officers who served the king in every matter concerning the divisions on rotating military duty each month throughout the year. There were 24,000 men in each division:
27.2: Jashobeam (the people will return) son of Zabdiel (God is my endowment) was in charge of the first division, which was assigned the first month. There were 24,000 men in his division.
27.3: He was a descendant of Perez (breaking out) and chief of all the army commanders for the first month.
27.4: Dodai (Dodo) the Ahohite (brother of rest) was in charge of the division for the second month, and Mikloth (staves) was the leader. There were 24,000 men in his division.
27.5: The third army commander, as chief for the third month, was Benaiah (YHWH has built up) son of Jehoiada (YHWH knows) the priest. There were 24,000 men in his division.
27.6: This Benaiah (YHWH has built up) was mighty among the Thirty and was over the Thirty, and his son Ammizabad (my people have bestowed) was in charge of his division.
27.7: The fourth, for the fourth month, was Joab’s (YHWH is father) brother Asahel (God-made), and his son Zebadiah (endowment of YHWH) was commander after him. There were 24,000 men in his division.
27.8: The fifth, for the fifth month, was the commander Shamhuth (Shammah) the Izrahite. There were 24,000 men in his division.
27.9: The sixth, for the sixth month, was Ira (watchful of a city) son of Ikkesh (twisted) the Tekoite. There were 24,000 men in his division.
27.10: The seventh, for the seventh month, was Helez (he has saved) the Pelonite (a certain one), an Ephraimite (doubly fruitful). There were 24,000 men in his division.
27.11: The eighth, for the eighth month, was Sibbecai (weaver) the Hushathite, a Zerahite. There were 24,000 men in his division.
27.12: The ninth, for the ninth month, was Abiezer (my father is help) the Anathothite, a Benjamite (son of the right hand). There were 24,000 men in his division.
27.13: The tenth, for the tenth month, was Maharai (impetuous) the Netophathite, a Zerahite. There were 24,000 men in his division.
27.14: The eleventh, for the eleventh month, was Benaiah (YHWH has built up) the Pirathonite, an Ephraimite (doubly fruitful). There were 24,000 men in his division.
27.15: The twelfth, for the twelfth month, was Heldai (Heled) the Netophathite, from the family of Othniel (lion of God). There were 24,000 men in his division.
27.16: These officers were in charge of the tribes of Israel (he wrestles with God): Over the Reubenites was Eliezer (God is my helper) son of Zichri (memorable); over the Simeonites was Shephatiah (YHWH has judged) son of Maacah (oppression);
27.17: over Levi (joined to) was Hashabiah (YHWH has considered) son of Kemuel (raised of God); over Aaron (light bringer) was Zadok (righteous);
27.18: over Judah (praised) was Elihu (He is my God), one of David’s (beloved) brothers; over Issachar (there is recompense) was Omri (pupil of YHWH) son of Michael (who is like God);
27.19: over Zebulun (exalted) was Ishmaiah (YHWH will hear) son of Obadiah (servant of YHWH); over Naphtali (wrestling) was Jerimoth (He is Most High) son of Azriel (my help is God);
27.20: over the Ephraimites (doubly fruitful) was Hoshea (salvation) son of Azaziah (YHWH is mighty); over one of the half-tribes of Manasseh (causing to forget) was Joel (YHWH is God) son of Pedaiah (YHWH has ransomed);
27.21: over the half-tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget) in Gilead (rocky region) was Iddo (timely / lovely) son of Zechariah (YHWH remembers); over Benjamin (son of the right hand) was Jaasiel (God is maker) son of Abner (my father is a lamp);
27.22: and over Dan (He has judged) was Azarel (God has helped) son of Jeroham (showing pity). These were the leaders of the tribes of Israel (he wrestles with God).
27.23: David (beloved) did not count the men aged twenty or under, because the LORD {YHWH} had said that He would make Israel (he wrestles with God) as numerous as the stars of the sky.
27.24: Joab (YHWH is father) son of Zeruiah (balsam) began to count the men but did not finish. For because of this census wrath came upon Israel (he wrestles with God), and the number was not entered in the Book of the Chronicles of King David (beloved).
27.25: Azmaveth (strong unto death) son of Adiel (ornament of God) was in charge of the royal storehouses. Jonathan (YHWH has given) son of Uzziah (my strength is YHWH) was in charge of the storehouses in the country, in the cities, in the villages, and in the fortresses.
27.26: Ezri (help of YHWH) son of Chelub (caged) was in charge of the workers in the fields who tilled the soil.
27.27: Shimei (renowned) the Ramathite was in charge of the vineyards. Zabdi (endowment) the Shiphmite was in charge of the produce of the vineyards for the wine vats.
27.28: Baal-hanan (Baal is gracious) the Gederite (a wall) was in charge of the olive and sycamore trees in the foothills {Shephelah}. Joash (YHWH hastens) was in charge of the stores of olive oil.
27.29: Shitrai (my officers) the Sharonite was in charge of the herds grazing in Sharon (a plain). Shaphat (He has judged) son of Adlai (justice of YHWH) was in charge of the herds in the valleys.
27.30: Obil (camel driver) the Ishmaelite was in charge of the camels. Jehdeiah (YHWH is unity) the Meronothite (joyful shouter) was in charge of the donkeys.
27.31: Jaziz (He makes prominent) the Hagrite (wanderer) was in charge of the flocks. All these officials were in charge of King David’s (beloved) property.
27.32: David’s (beloved) uncle Jonathan (YHWH has given) was a counselor; he was a man of insight and a scribe. Jehiel (God lives) son of Hachmoni (wise) attended to the sons of the king.
27.33: Ahithophel (my brother is folly) was the king’s counselor. Hushai (hastening) the Archite (lengthy) was the king’s friend.
27.34: Ahithophel (my brother is folly) was succeeded by Jehoiada (YHWH knows) son of Benaiah (YHWH has built up), then by Abiathar (my father is great). Joab (YHWH is father) was the commander of the king’s army.

28.1: Now David (beloved) summoned all the leaders of Israel (he wrestles with God) to Jerusalem (city of peace): the leaders of the tribes, the leaders of the divisions in the king’s service, the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, and the officials in charge of all the property and cattle of the king and his sons, along with the court officials and mighty men—every mighty man of valor.
28.2: Then King David (beloved) rose to his feet and said, “Listen to me, my brothers and my people. It was in my heart to build a house as a resting place for the ark of the covenant of the LORD {YHWH} and as a footstool for our God {Elohenu}. I had made preparations to build it,
28.3: but God {Elohim} said to me, ‘You are not to build a house for My Name, because you are a man of war who has shed blood.
28.4: Yet the LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God), chose me out of all my father’s house to be king over Israel forever. For He chose Judah (praised) as leader, and from the house of Judah He chose my father’s household, and from my father’s sons He was pleased to make me king over all Israel.
28.5: And of all my sons—for the LORD {YHWH} has given me many sons—He has chosen Solomon (peace) my son to sit on the throne of the kingdom of the LORD over Israel (he wrestles with God).
28.6: And He said to me, ‘Solomon (peace) your son is the one who will build My house and My courts, for I have chosen him as My son, and I will be his Father.
28.7: I will establish his kingdom forever, if he resolutely carries out My commandments and ordinances, as is being done this day.
28.8: So now in the sight of all Israel (he wrestles with God), the assembly of the LORD {YHWH}, and in the hearing of our God {Elohenu}, keep and seek out all the commandments of the LORD your? God, so that you? may possess this good land and leave it as an inheritance to your? descendants forever.
28.9: As for you, Solomon (peace) my son, know the God {Elohe} of your father and serve Him wholeheartedly and with a willing mind, for the LORD {YHWH} searches every heart and understands the intent of every thought. If you seek Him, He will be found by you; but if you forsake Him, He will reject you forever.
28.10: Consider now that the LORD {YHWH} has chosen you to build a house for the sanctuary. Be strong and do it.
28.11: Then David (beloved) gave his son Solomon (peace) the plans for the portico of the temple, its buildings, storehouses, upper rooms, inner rooms, and the room for the mercy seat (atonement cover).
28.12: The plans contained everything David had in mind {ruach} for the courts of the house of the LORD {YHWH}, for all the surrounding rooms, for the treasuries of the house of God {Elohim} and of the dedicated things,
28.13: for the divisions of the priests and Levites, for all the work of service in the house of the LORD {YHWH}, and for all the articles of service in the house of the LORD:
28.14: the weight of all the gold articles for every kind of service; the weight of all the silver articles for every kind of service;
28.15: the weight of the gold lampstands and their lamps, including the weight of each lampstand and its lamps; the weight of each silver lampstand and its lamps, according to the use of each lampstand;
28.16: the weight of gold for each table of showbread, and of silver for the silver tables;
28.17: the weight of the pure gold for the forks, sprinkling bowls, and pitchers; the weight of each gold dish; the weight of each silver bowl;
28.18: the weight of the refined gold for the altar of incense; and the plans for the chariot of the gold cherubim that spread their wings and overshadowed the ark of the covenant of the LORD {YHWH}.
28.19: “All this,” said David, “all the details of this plan, the LORD {YHWH} has made clear to me in writing by His hand upon me.
28.20: David also (beloved) said to Solomon (peace) his son, “Be strong and courageous, and do it. Do not be afraid or discouraged, for the LORD God {YHWH Elohim}, my God, is with you. He will neither fail you nor forsake you before all the work for the service of the house of the LORD is finished.
28.21: The divisions of the priests and Levites are ready for all the service of the house of God {Elohim}, and every willing man of every skill will be at your disposal for the work. The officials and all the people are fully at your command.

29.1: Then King David (beloved) said to the whole assembly, “My son Solomon (peace), the one whom God {Elohim} has chosen, is young and inexperienced. The task is great because this palace is not for man, but for the LORD {YHWH} God.
29.2: Now with all my ability I have made provision for the house of my God {Elohay}—gold for the gold articles, silver for the silver, bronze for the bronze, iron for the iron, and wood for the wood, as well as onyx for the settings, turquoise, stones of various colors, all kinds of precious stones, and slabs of marble—all in abundance.
29.3: Moreover, because of my delight in the house of my God {Elohay}, I now give for it my personal treasures of gold and silver, over and above all that I have provided for this holy temple:
29.4: three thousand talents of gold <113 t / 103 mt> (the gold of Ophir (reducing to ashes)) and seven thousand talents of refined silver <264 t / 239.5 mt>, to overlay the walls of the buildings,
29.5: for the gold work and the silver work, and for all the work to be done by the craftsmen. Now who is willing to consecrate himself to the LORD {YHWH} today?
29.6: Then the leaders of the families, the officers of the tribes of Israel (he wrestles with God), the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, and the officials in charge of the king’s work gave willingly.
29.7: Toward the service of God’s house they gave 5,000 talents <188.5 t / 171 mt> and 10,000 darics of gold <185.2 lb / 84 kg>, 10,000 talents of silver <377 t / 342 mt>, 18,000 talents of bronze <678.6 t / 615.6 mt>, and 100,000 talents of iron <3,770 t / 3,420 mt>.
29.8: Whoever had precious stones gave them to the treasury of the house of the LORD {YHWH}, under the care of Jehiel (Jehieli) the Gershonite.
29.9: And the people rejoiced at the willing response of their leaders, for they had given to the LORD freely {YHWH} and wholeheartedly. And King David (beloved) also rejoiced greatly.
29.10: Then David (beloved) blessed the LORD {YHWH} in the sight of all the assembly and said: “May You be blessed, O LORD, God {YHWH Elohe} of our father Israel (he wrestles with God), from everlasting to everlasting.
29.11: Yours, O LORD {YHWH}, is the greatness and the power and the glory and the splendor and the majesty, for everything in heaven and on earth belongs to You. Yours, O LORD, is the kingdom, and You are exalted as head over all.
29.12: Both riches and honor come from You, and You are the ruler over all. In Your hands are power and might to exalt and give strength to all.
29.13: Now therefore, our God {Elohenu}, we give You thanks, and we praise Your glorious name.
29.14: But who am I, and who are my people, that we should be able to give as generously as this? For everything comes from You, and from Your own hand we have given to You.
29.15: For we are foreigners and strangers in Your presence, as were all our forefathers. Our days on earth are like a shadow, without hope.
29.16: O LORD our God {YHWH Elohenu}, from Your hand comes all this abundance that we have provided to build You a house for Your holy Name, and all of it belongs to You.
29.17: I know, my God {Elohay}, that You test the heart and delight in uprightness. All these things I have given willingly and with an upright heart, and now I have seen Your people who are present here giving joyfully and willingly to You.
29.18: O LORD, God {YHWH Elohe} of our fathers Abraham (father of many), Isaac (he laughs), and Israel (he wrestles with God), keep this desire forever in the intentions of the hearts of Your people, and direct their hearts toward You.
29.19: And give my son Solomon (peace) a whole heart to keep and carry out all Your commandments, decrees, and statutes, and to build Your palace for which I have made provision.
29.20: Then David (beloved) said to the whole assembly, “Bless the LORD your? God {YHWH Eloheikem}. So the whole assembly blessed the LORD, the God of their fathers. They bowed down and paid homage to the LORD and to the king.
29.21: The next day they offered sacrifices {YHWH} and presented burnt offerings to the LORD: a thousand bulls, a thousand rams, and a thousand lambs, along with their drink offerings, and other sacrifices in abundance for all Israel (he wrestles with God).
29.22: That day they ate and drank with great joy in the presence of the LORD {YHWH}. Then, for a second time, they designated David’s (beloved) son Solomon (peace) as king, anointing him before the LORD as ruler, and Zadok (righteous) as the priest.
29.23: So Solomon (peace) sat on the throne of the LORD {YHWH} as king in place of his father David (beloved). He prospered, and all Israel (he wrestles with God) obeyed him.
29.24: All the officials and mighty men, as well as all of King David’s (beloved) sons, pledged their allegiance to King Solomon (peace).
29.25: The LORD {YHWH} highly exalted Solomon (peace) in the sight of all Israel (he wrestles with God) and bestowed on him royal majesty such as had not been bestowed on any king in Israel before him.
29.26: David (beloved) son of Jesse (I possess) was king over all Israel (he wrestles with God).
29.27: The length of David’s reign over Israel (he wrestles with God) was forty years—seven years in Hebron (association) and thirty-three years in Jerusalem (city of peace).
29.28: He died at a ripe old age, full of years, riches, and honor, and his son Solomon (peace) reigned in his place.
29.29: Now the acts of King David (beloved), from first to last, are indeed written in the Chronicles of Samuel (his name is El) the Seer, the Chronicles of Nathan (giver) the Prophet, and the Chronicles of Gad (troop) the Seer,
29.30: together with all the details of his reign, his might, and the circumstances that came upon him and Israel (he wrestles with God) and all the kingdoms of the lands.
